The video opens with a close-up of Oliver Tuszik, President of Cisco EMEA, standing on a dark stage, holding a microphone and smiling warmly as he speaks with an enthusiastic, slightly accented voice. He is dressed in a gray suit over a white shirt, with a full beard and expressive gestures. The stage is illuminated by vertical neon light strips in red, blue, and purple, creating a modern, tech-forward ambiance, with the 'Cisco Live!' logo visible in the top right corner. Tuszik declares Cisco Live! is "even bigger than before," with "more than 16,000 participants" from "more than 99 countries," emphasizing the "diversity of the region." As the camera pulls back, the full scale of the auditorium is revealed: a large, dimly lit venue with rows of seated attendees, many wearing lanyards, and large screens displaying Tuszik’s image from multiple angles. He walks confidently down the center aisle, engaging with the audience, who are visibly attentive and some are clapping audibly. The lighting shifts dynamically, with spotlights following him as he moves, enhancing the energetic and interactive atmosphere. He stops at a marked square on the floor, continuing to speak passionately, gesturing with his free hand, explaining that Cisco Live! is "our event to talk about the news, share the technology innovation, and to learn from each other," and also to "meet some good old friends and make some new friends," noting "more than 4,000 first-timers." The audience, a mix of professionals in business attire, watches intently, some recording the moment on their phones. Tuszik concludes by thanking the attendees and mentioning a group of "press and analysts" right behind him. The overall mood is dynamic and immersive, emphasizing connection and innovation, consistent with the Cisco Live! brand.

The video opens with a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium, bathed in blue stage lighting, where Oliver Tuszik, President of Cisco EMEA, walks confidently down the central aisle toward the audience. Dressed in a sharp gray suit and white shirt, holding a microphone, he engages the crowd with expressive hand gestures. His voice, clear and confident with a slight accent, immediately fills the space, thanking attendees for joining, sharing news, and for their critical feedback that helps Cisco improve daily. Subtle background music plays beneath his speech, adding to the event's atmosphere. The circular, elevated stage is surrounded by rows of seated attendees, many of whom are clapping or recording the event on their phones, their active engagement evident as Oliver addresses "all our amazing customers in this room," expressing his honor to have them. Large, curved screens above the stage display live feeds of Oliver from multiple angles, enhancing the immersive experience. As he moves along the aisle, spotlights follow him, creating dramatic flares and emphasizing his presence. Vertical neon light strips in red, blue, and purple line the stage perimeter, adding to the high-tech ambiance. The Cisco Live! logo is consistently visible in the top right corner, and a green banner briefly appears with the text 'Live from the Keynote.' Oliver pauses at a marked stage area—a square black mat with white borders—where he continues speaking passionately, gesturing toward the audience. He acknowledges the participants of the executive symposium, looking forward to an interesting discussion, and extends a warm welcome to "all our amazing partners that make us successful around the world." He then makes a special call out to a group of people—those with partners, customers, and Cisco—who "make Cisco technology work," emphasizing their daily efforts in delivering the best results, identifying them as "our technical communities, all our SEs, the software..." The camera alternates between wide-angle views capturing the scale of the venue and closer shots that highlight his animated delivery and the audience’s active participation, all underscored by his dynamic address.

Oliver Tuszik, President of Cisco EMEA, energetically walks down the aisle of a large, dimly lit auditorium, dressed in a gray suit with a white shirt, holding a microphone. He engages with the attentive audience, some clapping and others recording with smartphones, as he passionately declares, "We will fight hard to deserve this every single day. But with all of you, it will be no problem to continue the journey. Nothing can stop us. We are ready. So, let's go." The stage is illuminated with dynamic blue spotlights and vertical neon light strips in red, blue, and purple, creating a high-tech ambiance. As Oliver moves toward the center stage, the camera pans to reveal a massive curved screen displaying his live image from multiple angles, then transitioning to a colorful graphic with his name and title. Upbeat, energetic electronic music with a strong beat begins to play, accompanying his confident walk across the stage. The 'Cisco Live!' logo is consistently visible in the top right corner, and a green banner in the top left corner reads 'Live from the Keynote.' The music fades as Oliver continues, expressing his excitement: "So good to be back in EMEA, in this region. And as you can see, I'm I'm pretty excited. And there's a reason why I'm excited because I have an advantage. I've been five years abroad, living in California, working for Cisco, running the global partner and roots to market team. And I was talking to customers all around the world." The atmosphere remains electric, with the audience fully immersed in his keynote presentation.

The video opens with a close-up of Oliver Tuszik, President of Cisco EMEA, on a dark stage illuminated by vertical neon light strips in purple and blue. Dressed in a gray suit over a light blue shirt, he speaks passionately with a confident demeanor, gesturing with his right hand, discussing how the world in Europe and EMEA "looks much better than we sometimes think" and is "not the same place as when I left it five years ago." As the camera pulls back to reveal a wide, circular stage in a large auditorium filled with an engaged audience, many recording on their phones, Oliver emphasizes that "digital transformation is accelerating, no matter where we look," highlighting "mega projects" in the Middle East and their unprecedented "speed, the power." The presentation then transitions to an aerial view of the Santiago Bernabéu Stadium, showcasing its modern architecture, as Oliver speaks about Madrid's "Real Madrid" stadium being "one of the best, if not the best soccer stadium ever," "fully digitized," focused on the "best fan experience," and "supported by the full Cisco stack." This is followed by a shot of the Eiffel Tower, symbolizing global connectivity, as he announces Cisco's role as a "proud sponsor" of the "Paris Olympics," helping "secure the games." The video then returns to Oliver Tuszik, holding a remote and continuing his presentation with expressive hand movements, as the stage lighting subtly shifts. The Cisco Live! logo remains consistently visible in the top right corner, reinforcing the event’s branding, as Oliver concludes by noting that while "everybody talks about" these "big events," they are not the only focus. The atmosphere remains energetic and immersive, blending live presentation with high-tech visuals to emphasize Cisco’s global reach and innovation.

The video opens with a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium, bathed in blue and purple ambient lighting, filled with an engaged audience. On the circular stage, Oliver Tuszik, President of Cisco EMEA, dressed in a gray suit over a light blue shirt, walks confidently while speaking passionately into a headset microphone. His clear, accented voice declares, "It's what's happening in all industries, no matter where you look. All industries are driving the transformation." As he gestures expressively, emphasizing points about hearing from customers like L'Oréal and manufacturing companies, the massive curved video screen above displays dynamic visuals, including industrial scenes and railway tracks, visually reinforcing his mention of "critical infrastructure railways." He continues, highlighting HSBC's role in driving application performance and the importance of service providers and governments in digital transformation. The stage is surrounded by vertical neon light strips in purple and blue, and the screen also shows close-ups of Oliver and a 5G-enabled streetlight against a city skyline, transitioning between multiple panels. The attentive audience watches intently, some recording with smartphones, as the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains visible, underscoring the immersive, high-tech keynote presentation.

Oliver Tuszik, President of Cisco EMEA, confidently strides across a dark, modern stage, illuminated by vertical neon light strips in purple and blue, creating a high-tech ambiance. Dressed in a gray suit over a light blue shirt, with a full beard and a lapel microphone, he holds a small remote as he addresses the audience. The camera alternates between wide shots showing the vast, attentive audience in dim blue lighting, some recording on their phones, and close-ups capturing Tuszik's expressive hand gestures and engaging demeanor. A large curved video screen behind him displays dynamic visuals, including abstract blue waveforms and text overlays such as 'Technology is key to managing the challenges' and 'Technology is driving the disruption.' Tuszik passionately declares that "digitization all across the countries we are in. It's clear, technology is key to manage all this challenge. Without technology, we wouldn't be able to manage the challenges we're facing all around the world. But to be also very clear, technology itself is a driver of some of this disruptions. And yes, I'm talking about the biggest disruption ever, AI." He continues, explaining that attendees will "hear a lot about AI," detailing how Cisco helps build infrastructure to run AI securely, how they've been embedding AI into their products for 10-12 years, and their commitment to responsibly utilizing its power. He invites the audience to join him tomorrow to learn more about AI as "the biggest disruption ever." The Cisco Live! logo is consistently displayed in the top right corner, with a green 'Live from the Keynote' banner appearing briefly, emphasizing the live, professional, and immersive atmosphere.

The video opens with a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium at Cisco Live!, where Oliver Tuszik, President of Cisco EMEA, stands on a circular stage. Behind him, a massive curved video screen prominently displays 'Technology is driving the disruption' against a blue abstract background, while dynamic blue and purple neon lights illuminate the stage. Oliver, a bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, speaks passionately into a microphone with a clear, confident, and slightly accented voice, discussing how "Technology is driving the disruption" and emphasizing that "IT is the one that is changing everything." He moves dynamically across the stage, engaging the audience with expressive gestures, explaining that IT is "no longer a cost center only" and that CIOs "need to help to drive the business." As he speaks, the screen behind him transitions from mirroring his image to showcasing industrial scenes, 5G streetlights, railway tracks, and cityscapes, overlaid with colorful abstract shapes, visually reinforcing his points about IT being "key" and a "solution." He highlights the significant growth in the "ICT market," predicting it to be "four to five times bigger" than regional GDP growth, because "companies even invest in times" of change. The 'Cisco Live!' logo remains visible in the top right, and a 'Live from the Keynote' banner in the top left, underscoring the immersive, high-tech atmosphere of innovation.

event_blocks.summary: Oliver Tuszik, President of Cisco EMEA, stood on a dark stage illuminated by vertical purple and blue neon light strips, wearing a gray suit and light blue shirt, addressing a large, engaged audience in a modern auditorium. He spoke passionately, emphasizing that businesses will only survive by utilizing technology, expressing optimism for the future despite global challenges like wars, conflicts, energy costs, cost of living, global warming, and unstable political systems. Tuszik highlighted Cisco's "best portfolio ever," noting it is more integrated, open, automated, secure, and AI-powered, while acknowledging ongoing innovation and the need for time to achieve perfection. He discussed Cisco's strategy of driving mergers and acquisitions, specifically mentioning the "game-changing" acquisition of Splunk, and credited partners for helping implement solutions. Tuszik engaged the audience with expressive hand gestures and dynamic movement across the stage, pausing on a marked black mat with white borders. The large curved video screen above displayed multiple angles of his presentation, abstract shapes, industrial imagery, and later, presentation slides detailing "Acquisitions 2023" and key questions for the future, such as upgrading infrastructure for AI, ensuring security, user experience, collaboration, sustainability, and digital transformation. The atmosphere was high-tech and immersive, enhanced by dynamic spotlights, ambient lighting, and the consistent presence of the "Cisco Live!" logo and a "Live from the Keynote" banner.

Oliver Tuszik.description: A bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, holding a microphone, speaking passionately and articulately on a dark stage, gesturing expressively as he moves dynamically. He discusses adapting to global needs, upgrading infrastructure for AI, ensuring security, creating the best application experience, empowering users in a hybrid world, driving sustainability, and leveraging these for transformation, framing these as the key agenda items for the event.

Curved Video Screen Display.description: A large, multi-panel curved video screen above the stage that displays the speaker’s image and later transitions to a presentation slide titled 'Acquisitions 2023,' listing various company logos, enhancing the presentation’s visual impact.

Cisco Live!.description: The event branding visible in the top right corner of the screen, with a stylized logo and consistent presence throughout the segment, reinforcing the event's identity.

Stage Lighting Setup.description: Vertical neon light strips in purple and blue, along with dynamic spotlights and large screens displaying the speaker’s image from multiple angles, creating a high-tech, immersive environment.

Live from the Keynote Banner.description: A green, semi-transparent banner that appears in the top left corner of the screen, displaying the text 'Live from the Keynote' in white, indicating the live nature and importance of the session.

Audience Members.description: A diverse group of attendees seated in rows, many wearing lanyards with badges, dressed in business casual to formal attire, actively watching and reacting to the speaker; some are clapping, others recording with smartphones, indicating high engagement.

Marked Stage Area.description: A square black mat with white borders on the floor, positioned in the center aisle, where the speaker pauses to address the audience, serving as a focal point for interaction.

raw_segments: The video opens with a close-up of Oliver Tuszik, President of Cisco EMEA, standing on a dark stage with a deep blue backdrop, wearing a gray suit over a light blue shirt. He speaks passionately, using expressive hand gestures including pointing and open-palmed movements, with a small microphone clipped to his lapel. His voice, clear and carrying a distinct European accent, emphasizes that businesses are 'struggling' and will 'only survive' when they 'utilize the power of technology.' The 'Cisco Live!' logo is visible in the top right corner. Oliver expresses optimism, stating he's 'very optimistic' and 'bullish and positive for the future,' but quickly adds, 'I'm not stupid. I see what is happening,' highlighting global issues like 'two wars, conflicts, and a lot of innocent people are suffering,' along with 'energy costs, cost of living, global warming, unstable political systems, and deglobalization.' As he speaks, the camera pulls back to reveal a wide-angle view of the large auditorium, showing a circular stage illuminated by vertical neon light strips in purple and blue, with a massive, engaged audience seated in rows under dim, atmospheric lighting. A large curved video screen above displays multiple angles of Oliver’s presentation, interspersed with abstract shapes and industrial imagery. He walks confidently across the stage, engaging the audience with dynamic movement and continued gesturing, then moves down the central aisle, pausing on a marked black mat with white borders. The audience, visible in the foreground, appears captivated, with some recording on smartphones. Oliver emphasizes that 'no matter what is happening, we have an opportunity with IT to help,' expressing pride in 'a lot of you, our customers and partners' for 'making this a more inclusive and a better place.' The overall atmosphere is high-tech and immersive, enhanced by dynamic spotlights and the large screen visuals. A green 'Live from the Keynote' banner appears in the top left corner, reinforcing the live nature of the event, as Oliver concludes by stating, 'Let me get to the most important part of Cisco Live!, which is talking about technology. We love technology. There's no question. It's all about technology for us.'

The video opens with a close-up of Oliver Tuszik, President of Cisco EMEA, a bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, speaking passionately on a dark stage illuminated by vertical neon light strips in purple and blue. He gestures expressively with his hands as he moves across the stage, engaging the audience. Oliver, who states he has been "more than 10 years with Cisco" and "approximately 20 years working with Cisco" as an engineer, declares with conviction, "this is the best portfolio ever." He elaborates that "It's more integrated, it's more open, it's more automated," and "It's more secure and it's more AI powered than ever before." He acknowledges, "Is it perfect? No, of course not," explaining that "it will take some time to be perfect" and that Cisco is "continuously driving innovation." The camera pulls back to reveal a wide, circular stage with a large curved video screen above, displaying his image from multiple angles, enhancing the immersive, high-tech atmosphere. The audience, seated in rows, is visible in the foreground, attentively watching. As he continues, the screen behind him transitions to a presentation slide titled 'Acquisitions 2023,' listing companies such as ACEDIAN, Armorblox, Code BGP, ISOVALENT, Lightspin, OORT, SamKnows, smartlook, splunk>, VALTIX, and wgtwo. Oliver emphasizes that Cisco will "also continue to drive mergers and acquisitions," specifically mentioning that "the acquisitions that we did over the last year," including "the last big one about Splunk, will be game-changing for all of you." He adds that they are "getting stronger and stronger" and highlights the role of "all our partners that help us to implement the best solutions." The stage lighting remains dynamic, with spotlights and ambient blue and purple hues, while the 'Cisco Live!' logo is consistently displayed in the top right corner, reinforcing the event's identity in the large, modern auditorium.

The video opens with a close-up of Oliver Tuszik, a bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, speaking passionately on a dark stage with vertical neon light strips in purple and blue. He gestures expressively with his hands as he moves across the stage, engaging the audience, his voice clear and articulate as he discusses adapting to local needs and hearing the same topics from customers globally. He emphasizes key questions: "How can we upgrade the infrastructure? How can we make it ready for AI in the future world?" The camera pulls back to reveal a wide shot of the stage, showing the large audience seated in rows, many recording with smartphones, under dim blue lighting. Tuszik pauses on a marked black mat with white borders, continuing to address the crowd about making things "secure while we're upgrading it" and focusing on "the application experience." The presentation transitions to a large curved video screen displaying bold white text questions, mirroring Tuszik's speech: 'How do we upgrade our infrastructure for this new world?', 'How do we stay secure in the new world?', 'How do we ensure a great experience for users?', 'How do we create the best collaboration experience?', 'How do we address sustainability targets?', and 'And how do we leverage all of this to transform?' He reiterates the importance of empowering users in a hybrid world and driving sustainability, stating these questions form "the agenda of today." The Cisco Live! logo is consistently visible in the top right corner, and a green 'Live from the Keynote' banner appears in the top left, enhancing the dynamic and immersive atmosphere with spotlights and large screens.

event_blocks.summary: Oliver Tuszik introduced Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, at Cisco Live! with enthusiastic applause and upbeat electronic music. Davidson, on a circular stage illuminated by dynamic blue and purple vertical neon light strips, discussed digitalization, AI, and Cisco's role in advancing these technologies. He highlighted Cisco's commitment to simplifying and securing networking at every scale, powered by AI, and introduced Cisco as the 'Internet data company' with extensive data insights from over a billion endpoints, leveraging ThousandEyes for real-time monitoring and issue resolution. The presentation included a montage of global imagery, a Formula 1 pit stop analogy to illustrate speed and precision, and a demonstration of the ThousandEyes integration with Cisco Secure Access and Meraki dashboard. Davidson showcased the 'Experience Insights' dashboard, detailing endpoint performance metrics, and a path visualization graph demonstrating hop-by-hop network traffic analysis, pinpointing a service provider issue causing a brownout for a SaaS CRM application. The event emphasized Cisco's unified platform for managing network assets and providing deep visibility into the digital experience of users, applications, and devices.

Presentation Slide.description: A dark blue slide with white text listing strategic questions such as 'Simplifying and securing networking at every scale powered by AI' and 'Cisco is the Internet data company with billions of daily measurements,' displayed prominently behind Jonathan Davidson during his presentation.

Oliver Tuszik.description: A bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, holding a microphone, speaking passionately on a dark stage with vertical neon light strips in purple and blue; he gestures expressively and moves dynamically across the stage, engaging the audience with a warm demeanor, and is heard introducing Jonathan Davidson before being thanked by him.

Curved Video Screen Display.description: A large, multi-panel curved video screen above the stage that displays the speaker’s image and later transitions to a presentation slide titled 'Simplifying and securing networking at every scale,' enhancing the presentation’s visual impact.

Cisco Live!.description: The event branding visible in the top right corner of the screen, with a stylized logo, where Jonathan Davidson is speaking on stage, reinforcing the event's identity.

Stage Lighting Setup.description: Vertical neon light strips in purple, blue, red, and green, along with dynamic spotlights and large screens displaying the speaker’s image from multiple angles, creating a high-tech, immersive environment.

Global Montage.description: A sequence of symbolic visuals including a nighttime port with cranes and shipping containers, a close-up of a green sprout emerging from soil, and a crowded city street, used to illustrate themes of global connectivity, growth, and urban life.

Jonathan Davidson.description: A man with curly hair and glasses, wearing a dark blazer over a blue plaid shirt, gray trousers, and dark sneakers, speaking on stage with expressive hand gestures; identified as EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking. He explains the process of activating ThousandEyes, how the Meraki dashboard and path visualization reveal network issues, and specifically diagnoses 'user connections timing out because a service provider network... is dropping some traffic,' causing a 'brownout condition' outside of IT's control.

Audience Members.description: A diverse group of attendees seated in rows, actively watching and listening to Jonathan Davidson, whose questions and subsequent comments ("Nobody. That's great. That's amazing.") indicate their engagement and interaction, even if their direct verbal responses are not heard.

Formula 1 Pit Crew.description: A team of mechanics in bright orange uniforms and blue helmets, performing a rapid tire change on a racing car during a pit stop, with 'DP WORLD' and 'Cisco' branding visible on the car and backdrop. This dynamic footage is shown on the stage screens, visually illustrating Jonathan Davidson's analogy of driving a 'Formula 1 race car'.

Live from the Keynote Banner.description: A green, semi-transparent banner that appears in the top left corner of the screen, displaying the text 'Live from the Keynote' in white, indicating the live nature and importance of the session.

Experience Insights Dashboard.description: A detailed interface titled 'Experience Insights Powered by ThousandEyes,' displaying real-time data on endpoints including user names, locations, health status, latency, jitter, loss, CPU, and memory usage, with some endpoints marked as 'Healthy' and others as 'Offline.'

Marked Stage Area.description: A square black mat with white borders on the floor, positioned in the center aisle, where the speaker pauses to address the audience, serving as a focal point for interaction.

Configuration Interface.description: A user-friendly interface titled 'Monitor Critical Applications for SD-WAN,' showing steps to connect ThousandEyes, select applications like Salesforce, and configure monitoring, with a green header and clear navigation, where 'active application monitoring' is selected and 'ThousandEyes is activated on MX devices' for network visibility.

Path Visualization Graph.description: A detailed network trace diagram showing multiple agents (e.g., MX-Branch-1, MX-Branch-2) connecting to a target server (https://abc.salesforce.com/), with nodes color-coded by status (red for errors, green for success) and lines indicating data paths. Jonathan Davidson describes it as providing a 'hop-by-hop view' of network traffic, highlighting 'red circles' where 'user connections are timing out' due to a 'service provider network... dropping some traffic,' leading to a 'brownout condition.' A specific node 'xe-3-3-2-dcr1-lon.net.sfdc.net' is shown with metrics like 84% forwarding loss and 73.3 ms average response.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a close-up of a presentation slide displaying strategic questions in white text on a dark blue background, featuring the 'Cisco Live!' logo. An unseen speaker, later identified as Oliver Tuszik, is heard discussing topics on the main stage and breakouts, then introduces his 'first guest speaker,' initially stumbling over the title 'EVP and General Manager for Networking' before clearly announcing 'Jonathan Davidson!' This introduction is met with enthusiastic applause from the visible audience and the immediate start of upbeat, energetic electronic music. The scene transitions to a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium with a circular stage, where Oliver Tuszik, a bearded man in a gray suit, walks confidently, his image mirrored on a massive curved screen above. As Jonathan Davidson, identified by a lower-third graphic as EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, appears on stage in a dark blazer over a plaid shirt, the camera alternates between close-ups of him and wide shots of the stage, where the curved screen now displays his image and the Cisco Live! branding. Jonathan Davidson then speaks, thanking Oliver Tuszik for the 'great entrance' and expressing his excitement to be there. He discusses how 'digitalization has ignited our curiosity about productivity, inclusivity, and of course, convenience,' and how 'AI, as Oliver talked about, is fanning those flames and fueling all of our business growth.' He concludes by stating Cisco's commitment to taking digitalization and AI 'even further to help you.' Throughout his speech, he gestures expressively, while the stage is illuminated by dynamic blue and purple vertical neon light strips. The video then cuts to a montage of global imagery—a bustling port at night, a green sprout emerging from soil, and a crowded city street—symbolizing growth and connectivity, all while the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains consistently visible.

The minute opens with an aerial night view of a densely packed urban area, transitioning to a wide shot of a large, dark auditorium. On a circular stage, bathed in dramatic blue lighting, Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, speaks with a clear, engaging, and conversational voice. Dressed in a dark blazer over a plaid shirt and wearing glasses, he gestures expressively as he recounts, "It's been more than 25 years since I made my first IP telephony call, but I still remember the amazement that it provoked in me." He continues, describing it as "the beginning of a new era in communication," and draws parallels to the evolution of cloud technology, noting, "many of you might have felt the same way with cloud over the past 10 years." Large screens surrounding the stage display his image from multiple angles, enhancing the immersive experience for the attentive audience seated in rows. As Davidson emphasizes the symbiotic relationship between cloud and network, stating, "There is no AI without the network," the screens dynamically shift to display a presentation slide with this bold text against a green, leafy background. The scene then cuts to a high-energy Formula 1 pit stop sequence, showing a team in bright orange uniforms and blue helmets rapidly changing a tire on a racing car, prominently featuring 'DP WORLD' and 'Cisco' branding. This dynamic footage is displayed on the stage screens, illustrating Davidson's next point as he remarks, "You know, it's like driving a Formula 1 race car." He continues his presentation with animated hand gestures, reinforcing the themes of network-driven innovation, speed, and precision.

The video opens with a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium, showcasing a speaker, Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, on a circular stage under a massive curved video screen. The screen displays a vibrant, abstract background with the text 'Simplifying and securing networking at every scale.' Davidson, dressed in a dark blazer over a plaid shirt, gray trousers, and dark sneakers, speaks with a clear, confident American accent, gesturing expressively with both hands as he dynamically moves across the stage. He begins by engaging the audience, noting, "All right, so somebody does. One person, that's amazing," eliciting a brief, light chuckle from the attentive audience members visible in the foreground. Vertical neon light strips in red, blue, and green illuminate the stage, creating a modern, tech-forward ambiance. A green 'Live from the Keynote' banner is visible in the top left, while 'Cisco Live!' branding is in the top right. Davidson continues, using a driving metaphor to explain Cisco's role: "You are in control of something revolutionary as long as the car is not controlling you. You need a pit crew that can make split-second decisions for you. Cisco is your crew for AI." He elaborates, "Think of us as watching the road ahead for you. You can drive while we give you insights to avoid threats and anything that is going to slow you down." Directly aligning with the screen's text, he states, "To do this, we are simplifying and we are securing networking everywhere for everyone at every scale. That is the Cisco Networking Cloud." He then defines it as "an integrated platform for on-premise and for cloud operating models," allowing users to "manage all of the Cisco networking assets and products from one place."

Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, stands center stage at Cisco Live!, dressed in a dark blazer over a plaid shirt, gray trousers, and dark sneakers, speaking with expressive hand gestures to a large, engaged audience. He passionately declares that "fragmented platforms converge, swivel share operations are a thing of the past, hidden threats are surfaced and eliminated." The stage is dark with dynamic vertical neon light strips in red, blue, and green, creating a modern, tech-forward atmosphere. Behind him, a large curved video screen displays vibrant abstract graphics before transitioning to a presentation slide with the text 'Simplifying and securing networking at every scale powered by AI,' as Jonathan emphasizes, "And the platform is powered by AI to simplify, secure, and scale your operations across the entire network infrastructure." He continues, stating, "Now data makes all of this possible... Cisco is the Internet data company." The scene then cuts to a full-stage view showing the expansive auditorium, with the same slide, now displaying 'Cisco is the Internet data company with billions of daily measurements' alongside an aerial cityscape, across multiple screens. Many audience members in the foreground hold up phones to record. The camera returns to close-ups of Jonathan Davidson as he continues his presentation, using emphatic gestures and moving slightly across the stage, maintaining strong audience engagement, explaining, "With visibility into more than one billion end points... Cisco's data set is orders of magnitude better than anyone else in this industry." He then highlights, "ThousandEyes is taking billions of measurements every single day across cloud, data centers, service providers, and of course, across enterprise networks where you have enabled that. It is able to detect..." The 'Cisco Live!' logo remains consistently in the top right corner, reinforcing the event branding.

Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, opens the segment in a close-up, standing on a dark stage with a deep blue backdrop. Dressed in a dark blazer over a gray and white plaid shirt, gray trousers, and dark sneakers, he speaks with an authoritative and engaging voice, using expressive hand gestures to explain how to understand the digital experience of users, applications, and things, and most importantly, how to take action based on that data. The 'Cisco Live!' logo is visible in the top right corner. The camera then pulls back to reveal a wide, circular stage with a large, curved video screen above, displaying his image from multiple angles and a presentation slide stating 'Cisco is the Internet data company with billions of daily measurements' alongside a cityscape. The stage is illuminated by vibrant vertical neon light strips in purple, blue, red, and green, creating a high-tech, immersive atmosphere. The dimly lit audience, seated in rows, watches intently, with some clapping and others recording on smartphones. Jonathan continues, inviting the audience to look at ThousandEyes in action for branch and campus, providing insights into remote users, their individual devices, and the local area network. He then announces the 'ThousandEyes integration with Cisco Secure Access,' which appears on screen in bold white text on a blue and yellow gradient background. An audience member lets out a distinct 'woo,' which Jonathan acknowledges with enthusiasm, calling it 'great' and identifying the solution as their secure services edge. The presentation transitions to a detailed 'Experience Insights Powered by ThousandEyes' dashboard, showing real-time data on endpoints, including user names, locations, health status, latency, jitter, loss, CPU, and memory usage. The dashboard highlights several endpoints, some 'Healthy' with green checkmarks, others 'Offline,' and displays a 'Config' button and a timestamp from December 13, 2023. Jonathan points to the screen, explaining how security and IT now have one place to see the digital experience of employees anywhere, allowing immediate visibility into issues across the endpoint, wireless, service provider, and all applications.

The video opens with a close-up of Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, speaking passionately on stage at Cisco Live! He is dressed in a dark blazer over a blue plaid shirt, gray trousers, and dark sneakers, gesturing expressively with his hands as he asks the audience if they'd like to know about their CIO having issues before they do, then humorously notes, "Nobody. That's great. That's amazing." He continues, asking how they'd like to "roll back a change automatically when ThousandEyes sees a problem," asserting, "Well, now you can do that." The camera pulls back to reveal a wide shot of the stage, showing him standing on a marked black mat in the center aisle, addressing a large, engaged audience seated under dim, atmospheric lighting with vertical neon light strips in blue, red, and green illuminating the dark backdrop. As he emphasizes the focus on "the individual device with ThousandEyes Endpoint," the screen transitions to a bold announcement slide: 'ANNOUNCING ThousandEyes Endpoint,' set against a dynamic blue and yellow background. The video then cuts to a detailed 'Experience Insights' dashboard, powered by ThousandEyes, displaying real-time endpoint data including user names, locations, health status, latency, jitter, loss, CPU, and memory usage. Jonathan's voice explains that users "will see performance data" and "understand how you can look at any device and how that device impacts the applications and vice versa," which is "key for rapid issue resolution," eliminating "scattered information or specialized agents." Some endpoints are marked as 'Healthy' with green checkmarks, while others are 'Offline,' with the dashboard last updated on December 13, 2023. The scene returns to Jonathan Davidson, who continues his presentation with animated hand gestures, emphasizing that "ThousandEyes Endpoints puts you in control with deep visibility into specific system metrics." The camera alternates between close-ups of him and wide shots of the stage, capturing the audience's attention. The video then transitions to a clean, user-friendly configuration interface titled 'Monitor Critical Applications for SD-WAN,' with a green header, showing steps to connect ThousandEyes and select applications like Office 365 Suite, Cisco WebEx, Zoom, and Salesforce for monitoring. Jonathan's voice explains, "we also have integrated ThousandEyes into platforms that you already love, like Meraki dashboard," before the segment concludes with him still actively engaging the audience, reinforcing the integration of ThousandEyes technology into Cisco’s network monitoring solutions.

The segment opens with a user navigating a 'Monitor Critical Applications for SD-WAN' configuration interface, where they select 'MX Branch' to activate as a ThousandEyes agent for application testing, a process Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, describes as selecting "active application monitoring" and activating "ThousandEyes on your MX devices with a few clicks to have visibility into how the network is performing for users and for their applications." He adds, "I did it myself just last week on my own home network. It was very, very easy." The scene transitions to a dashboard listing 26 Enterprise Agents, then to a detailed path visualization graph. Davidson explains that if a site is inaccessible, the "Meraki dashboard can now show you why," and "Path visualization gives you a hop-by-hop view of the network traffic between the MX device and the destination of that traffic." The graph shows network traces from multiple agents to https://abc.salesforce.com/, with red nodes indicating errors. Davidson instructs, "Look at the red circles. User connections are timing out because a service provider network leading to the SaaS CRM application is dropping some traffic." A specific node, 'xe-3-3-2-dcr1-lon.net.sfdc.net,' is highlighted, showing 84% forwarding loss and 73.3 ms average response in London. Davidson clarifies, "Now, this is happening multiple hops away out of the control of IT. It is causing a brownout condition and it's owned by that third party." The final frames show a split-screen view with the path visualization on the left and Davidson on the right, speaking on stage at Cisco Live! He wears a dark blazer over a blue plaid shirt, gesturing expressively, with dynamic purple and blue neon lighting and the Cisco Live! logo visible. The presentation slide behind him reads 'Simplifying and securing networking at every scale,' reinforcing the theme of advanced network monitoring and troubleshooting.

event_blocks.summary: Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, appeared on stage wearing a dark blazer over a blue plaid shirt, gray trousers, and dark sneakers, gesturing expressively as he spoke with a clear, authoritative voice. He explained how the enterprise network team can fix issues by routing users to different paths, which keeps the workplace connected, makes for happy users, and saves IT 'literally hours of analysis.' A complex ThousandEyes network path visualization graph, showing multiple agents connecting to a target server with color-coded nodes indicating health status, was displayed beside him, which Davidson emphasized, stating, 'Only ThousandEyes can give you this level of detail.' He continued, detailing how they 'highlight wireless networks, identify real-time issues across multiple domains, and go deep into third-party SaaS application performance.' The vibrant, abstract rainbow-colored lighting in the background shifted as the scene transitioned to a bold announcement slide with a dark blue background and white text, declaring 'ANNOUNCING Cloud-managed Catalyst switching Cloud-monitored Catalyst wireless,' highlighted by a green 'ANNOUNCING' tag. Davidson's voice continued, introducing 'your journey to cloud management and monitoring of all your Cisco wired and wireless infrastructure,' explaining that 'Our highest performing Catalyst switching products are integrated into the Meraki dashboard to unify and to secure your experience and the end-user experience.' The presentation then shifted to a detailed Meraki dashboard interface, showing a list of switches with their status, MAC addresses, connectivity, serial numbers, and local IPs, with one switch marked as 'Alerting.' Davidson also explained how users can perform packet capture, visually demonstrated on a 'Packet Capture' interface showing fields for switch selection, ports, duration, and filter expressions, highlighting the simplicity of initiating 'a remote packet' capture with 'one click.' He detailed troubleshooting with CLI within the Meraki Dashboard and viewing iOS XC configuration changes over time, highlighting exactly what changed between versions via the 'Config History' tab with timestamps and a 'Compare changes' toggle. Davidson explained the ability to immediately apply or schedule firmware upgrades directly from the dashboard in three clicks for a few or tens of thousands of switches, coinciding with the 'Firmware upgrades' section and a 'Schedule upgrade' dialog appearing, followed by a 'Success' notification. The view shifted to a 'Switches' list showing device statuses and two 'Alerting' switches, then to an 'Add devices' interface. Davidson emphasized managing Catalyst infrastructure from the cloud, introducing the new Catalyst 9300M which can be managed by the Meraki Dashboard right out of the box. The 'Usage and clients' dashboard, displaying network usage and connected clients, was shown, followed by 'Wireless-Air' client details with a map and usage graph. He concluded by announcing the exciting ability to monitor Catalyst 9800 wireless access points and controllers from the cloud, all while the 'Cisco Live!' logo remained visible in the top right corner.

Presentation Slide.description: A dark blue slide with white text announcing 'ANNOUNCING Cloud-managed Catalyst switching Cloud-monitored Catalyst wireless' with a green tag, introduced as part of 'your journey to cloud management and monitoring of all your Cisco wired and wireless infrastructure.'

Path Visualization Graph.description: A detailed network trace diagram from ThousandEyes, showing multiple agents (e.g., MX-Branch-1, MX-Branch-2) connecting to a target server (https://abc.salesforce.com/), with nodes color-coded by status (red for errors, green for success) and lines indicating data paths, including a highlighted node 'xe-3-3-2-dcr1-lon.net.sfdc.net' with metrics like 84% forwarding loss and 73.3 ms average response; it is presented as a tool that provides 'this level of detail' to 'highlight wireless networks, identify real-time issues across multiple domains, and go deep into third-party SaaS application performance.'

Meraki Dashboard Interface.description: A user interface showing various networking management features, including a list of switches with their status, MAC addresses, connectivity, serial numbers, and local IPs, with one switch marked as 'Alerting' and a green header bar at the top, which Jonathan Davidson describes as a tool for troubleshooting with CLI, managing Catalyst infrastructure like the 9300M, and monitoring Catalyst 9800 wireless access points and controllers.

Jonathan Davidson.description: A man with curly hair and glasses, wearing a dark blazer over a blue plaid shirt, gray trousers, and dark sneakers, speaking on stage with expressive hand gestures; identified as EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, whose clear, enthusiastic voice explains the features of the Meraki Dashboard, including packet capture, configuration change tracking, firmware upgrades, and Catalyst infrastructure management.

Packet Capture Interface.description: A detailed interface for initiating a packet capture on a specific switch, with fields for switch selection, ports, duration, filter expressions, and a 'Start capture' button, along with sample filter expressions and a note on maximum capture duration, which Jonathan Davidson highlights as a key troubleshooting feature within the Meraki Dashboard.

Cisco Live!.description: The event branding visible in the top right corner of the screen, with a stylized logo and consistent presence throughout the segment, reinforcing the event's identity.

event_blocks.summary: The presentation begins with a male presenter in a dark blazer and glasses introducing the Meraki dashboard interface, which elicits a "woo" from the audience for its speed in analyzing and fixing network issues. A slide featuring L'Oréal, the "number one beauty company in the world," appears, accompanied by product application scenes. The presenter highlights L'Oréal's long-standing Cisco customer relationship and their adoption of the "platform approach and the Cisco networking cloud," praising the "100-year-old company embracing technology to transform." A wide shot reveals the presenter on a circular stage with blue stage lighting and large screens displaying the L'Oréal slide to a seated audience. A new male voice emphasizes IT's role in L'Oréal becoming the "number one beauty tech company," followed by a montage of L'Oréal's digital and manufacturing operations, including a hair analysis mobile app interface and automated production lines, with the voice detailing connectivity responsibilities across "research departments, factories, retail, headquarters." The scene shifts to an interview with Charles Croft, L'Oréal's Infrastructure / Network Services EMEA Network & Telecoms Manager, in a modern office, explaining how past network complexities have evolved into high-availability solutions with Cisco Meraki, encompassing network, Wi-Fi, sensors, and CCTV. A retail environment scene shows a woman interacting with a digital display and tablet at checkout, illustrating the customer experience. Charles demonstrates remote device control from his mobile phone, even while on a London double-decker bus, visually represented by a smartphone displaying real-time power metrics and the bus traversing city streets. Close-ups of Cisco networking hardware, including switches and an access point, are shown before returning to Charles, who explains how the technology simplifies operations, turning months-long setups into "plug-and-play processes" through automation and APIs, visually reinforced by a person at a desk with dual monitors displaying network management software. A graphic stating '3500 devices in 3 days' aligns with Charles's remark about patching that many devices in a short timeframe, with the 'Cisco Live!' logo visible throughout.

Charles Croft.description: A man with short hair, wearing a dark blazer over a floral shirt, speaking directly to the camera in a modern office setting with red and white chairs in the background, identified as Infrastructure / Network Services EMEA Network & Telecoms Manager at L'Oréal. He speaks clearly and professionally about the evolution from complex systems to high-availability solutions using Cisco Meraki as a connectivity platform, encompassing network, Wi-Fi, WAN, sensors, and CCTV. He highlights the ability to manage devices remotely via a dashboard and mobile phone, simplifying operations like setting up pop-ups from months to plug-and-play. He emphasizes the importance of automation and APIs, concluding by praising the rapid deployment of 3500 devices in 3 days.

Smartphone Interface.description: A hand holding a smartphone displaying a 'Wall screen' app with real-time power metrics such as voltage, current, and energy usage, visually demonstrating the capability Charles Croft describes: 'I can turn off any device anywhere in Europe... from my mobile phone. That's the power of having the dashboard and having the app.'

Deployment Graphic.description: A blue graphic with white text stating '3500 devices in 3 days,' surrounded by icons of connected devices, perfectly synchronizing with Charles Croft's enthusiastic declaration, 'Patching three and a half thousand devices in three days is fantastic.'

Retail Environment.description: A modern retail setting with a woman in a pink striped shirt interacting with a digital display and a tablet at a checkout counter, illustrating the seamless customer experience enabled by the 'connectivity platform' that Charles Croft describes as including 'sensors and CCTV.'

raw_segments: The video begins with a close-up of a Meraki dashboard interface, displaying network monitoring data, as a male presenter in a dark blazer and glasses gestures in a small inset window, stating, "the Meraki dashboard." He receives an audible "woo" from the audience, acknowledging the ability to "analyze" and "fix things so much faster" with the network. The scene transitions to a full-screen slide featuring L'Oréal with the tagline 'The number one beauty company in the world,' accompanied by a close-up of a woman’s face and a hand applying product. The presenter announces, "And one company on that journey is L'Oréal," noting their long-standing relationship as a Cisco customer and their embrace of the "platform approach and the Cisco networking cloud," calling it "beautiful to see a 100-year-old company embracing technology to transform" before instructing to "roll the video." The camera then pulls back to reveal a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium with blue stage lighting, where the same presenter stands on a circular stage, speaking to a seated audience, flanked by large screens displaying the L'Oréal slide. The presenter, now in a full-body shot, walks across the stage with expressive hand gestures, illuminated by vertical neon light strips. A new male voice begins speaking, identifying L'Oréal as "the largest beauty company in the world today" and emphasizing IT as an enabler to become "the number one beauty tech company." The video cuts to a close-up of a man applying a product to a woman’s hair, with the word 'SCAN' overlaid, followed by a montage of L'Oréal’s digital and manufacturing operations, including a mobile app interface for hair analysis and automated production lines. The voice continues, detailing L'Oréal's "research departments, factories, retail, headquarters," and his responsibility for "providing the connectivity and the networks in all of those locations." The scene shifts to an interview with Charles Croft, identified as Infrastructure / Network Services EMEA Network & Telecoms Manager at L'Oréal, who speaks directly to the camera in a modern office setting with red and white chairs in the background, continuing to explain his role. The video concludes with a final shot of Charles Croft speaking, with the Cisco Live! logo visible in the top right corner throughout.

The segment opens with Charles Croft, Infrastructure / Network Services EMEA Network & Telecoms Manager at L'Oréal, speaking directly to the camera in a modern office, explaining how past network complexities have evolved into high-availability solutions thanks to products like Cisco Meraki. He describes this as a comprehensive connectivity platform, extending beyond just network and Wi-Fi to include sensors and CCTV. As he speaks, the scene transitions to a retail environment where a woman seamlessly interacts with a digital display and a tablet at checkout, illustrating the customer experience enabled by such a platform. Charles continues, emphasizing the power of the dashboard and app, which allows him to remotely control any device in Europe from his mobile phone, a capability visually demonstrated by a hand using a smartphone to display real-time power metrics. He further illustrates this by mentioning managing devices from a London double-decker bus, which is shown traversing the city streets. The video then highlights the underlying technology with close-ups of Cisco networking hardware, including switches and a wall-mounted access point, before returning to Charles. He elaborates on how this technology simplifies operations, turning what used to be months-long pop-up setups into plug-and-play processes, underscoring the critical role of automation and APIs. A person is then shown working at a desk with dual monitors displaying network management software, visually representing these advanced capabilities. The segment culminates with a graphic proudly stating '3500 devices in 3 days,' perfectly aligning with Charles's enthusiastic remark about patching that many devices in such a short timeframe, before he delivers his final remarks. Throughout, the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains visible in the top right corner.

event_blocks.summary: Charles Croft, L'Oréal's Infrastructure / Network Services EMEA Network & Telecoms Manager, explained in a modern office setting that "doing things quicker makes it much simpler and cheaper to do," noting the Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) for their legacy systems versus Cisco Meraki systems is "about half," allowing them to "do more with less." The scene then shifted to a large, dimly lit Cisco Live! auditorium with a circular stage bathed in blue light, surrounded by a massive audience, many wearing lanyards and recording on smartphones, amidst dynamic purple, blue, red, and green vertical neon light strips. Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, walked onto the stage in a dark blazer over a blue plaid shirt and gray trousers, acknowledging the applause and stating, "That's wonderful." He announced that "both OpenAI and L'Oréal are rolling Meraki out across their entire enterprise infrastructure," and that Cisco is "redefining cloud-based secure connectivity for the branch and the campus by integrating the campus, the Catalyst SD-WAN, and the Cisco..." as a slide announced 'ANNOUNCING Secure Access with Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN.' Davidson then introduced "Cisco Secure Access, our secure services edge solution," explaining its application for "branch internet" and "SAS traffic." Demonstrating its ease of use, he showed the Cisco Secure Access Credential dialog box for API key and secret input, followed by the Policies page where he selected a 'US-West Branches' policy group with 'Embedded Security' set to 'Cisco_Secure_Access_Connectivity' and clicked 'Deploy,' receiving a 'Success' notification for deployment to "20 sites," emphasizing it enables "rapidly deploy scalable, highly secure, sassy-based architectures with just a few clicks." A graphic with 'Let’s go' and the Cisco Live! logo appeared briefly. Davidson continued, explaining that "the role of IT is expanding beyond cybersecurity," and how "customers count on Wi-Fi enabled, AI capable Meraki cameras for physical security," citing a "European rail operator" using them to "secure their own operations," all while moving dynamically across the stage with animated gestures under the enhanced high-tech atmosphere and multi-angle screen displays.

raw_segments: The video opens with a close-up of Charles Croft, Infrastructure / Network Services EMEA Network & Telecoms Manager at L'Oréal, speaking directly to the camera in a modern office setting. Dressed in a dark blazer over a floral shirt, he explains how "doing things quicker makes it much simpler and cheaper to do," noting that "the TCO of our legacy systems versus our Cisco Meraki systems, we reckon is about half." He emphasizes that "it costs us half to run exactly the same estate as it used to," concluding, "we do more with less." As he finishes, the scene transitions to a wide-angle view of a large, dimly lit auditorium at Cisco Live!, where a circular stage is illuminated by blue lighting and surrounded by a massive audience. Vertical neon light strips in purple, blue, red, and green enhance the dynamic, high-tech atmosphere. Applause and cheering erupt from the audience, many wearing lanyards, with some recording the event on their smartphones. Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, walks onto the stage, dressed in a dark blazer over a blue plaid shirt, gray trousers, and dark sneakers. He gestures expressively as he addresses the crowd, with large screens behind him displaying his image and the Cisco Live! branding. He acknowledges the applause, stating, "That's wonderful." He then highlights that "L'Oréal is not the only company that sees new possibilities with this dramatic simplification," mentioning that "both OpenAI and L'Oréal are rolling Meraki out across their entire enterprise infrastructure." He continues, "Now we are redefining cloud-based secure connectivity for the branch and the campus by integrating the campus, the Catalyst SD-WAN, and the Cisco..." as a bold announcement slide appears on the curved video screen above the stage, prominently displaying 'ANNOUNCING Secure Access with Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN,' signaling a major product reveal.

The minute opens with a wide shot of a dimly lit Cisco Live! auditorium, where Jonathan Davidson, a man in a dark blazer over a plaid shirt, stands center stage, illuminated by blue lights, addressing a massive audience. He speaks clearly, introducing "Cisco Secure Access, our secure services edge solution," explaining it works for "branch internet" and "SAS traffic." As he details how to "connect branches to the Cisco Secure Access across the SD-WAN fabric," the large curved video screen above him transitions to a software interface. This interface first displays the 'Cisco Secure Access Credential' dialog box for API key and secret input, followed by a 'Save' action. The screen then shows the 'Policies' page, highlighting the 'US-West Branches' policy group with 'Embedded Security' set to 'Cisco_Secure_Access_Connectivity'. Jonathan clicks 'Deploy', and a 'Success' notification confirms the policy was "deployed to 20 sites," which he states is "literally that easy" and enables "rapidly deploy scalable, highly secure, sassy-based architectures with just a few clicks." A vibrant graphic with 'Let’s go' and the Cisco Live! logo briefly appears before returning to Jonathan on stage. He continues his presentation, now with a multi-panel security dashboard displaying live surveillance footage on the large screen. He explains that "the role of IT is expanding beyond cybersecurity," and how "customers count on Wi-Fi enabled, AI capable Meraki cameras for physical security," citing a "European rail operator" who uses them to "secure their own operations." Throughout, Jonathan moves dynamically across the stage, engaging the audience with animated gestures, while vertical neon strips in red, blue, and green enhance the high-tech atmosphere, and the screens display his image from multiple angles.

event_blocks.summary: Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, explained the challenge of extracting meaningful insights from vast amounts of surveillance footage generated by thousands of 24/7 cameras, highlighting how AI, specifically within the Meraki Vision Interface, can process this data in seconds. The Meraki Vision dashboard, initially shown displaying a grid of thumbnails from a train station, features filters for time, cameras, people (with color options), and vehicles, demonstrating its capability to isolate specific events or individuals that would otherwise take days to find. Davidson, speaking live on stage at Cisco Live! amidst vibrant neon lighting and projected visuals, further elaborated on the interface's advanced filtering, including object recognition and color attributes, exemplified by the ability to find "the person in the red shirt." The system's application extends to critical infrastructure, as evidenced by a customer using Meraki cameras for rail operations and security, where AI trained to recognize employee uniforms can alert security if an unauthorized individual enters a restricted area. The Meraki Vision Interface's 'List View' mode was showcased with a specific incident detection: a person in an orange jacket on train tracks, underscoring the system's effectiveness in flagging potential safety hazards, all while the 'Cisco Live!' branding remained visible.

Meraki Vision Interface.description: A detailed user interface for Cisco Meraki Vision, initially showing a grid of surveillance thumbnails from a train station with filters for time, cameras, people (top and bottom clothing colors), and vehicles (type and color). It is demonstrated through Jonathan Davidson's explanation of its ability to filter on objects, use color attributes (e.g., 'find me the person in the red shirt'), and detect safety incidents, such as a person in an orange jacket on train tracks, by recognizing unauthorized entry into prohibited areas without employee uniforms. The interface also features sliders for 'Object 1' match precision and 'Minimum event duration', and is shown in both grid and 'List View' modes.

raw_segments: The video opens with a detailed view of the Cisco Meraki Vision dashboard, a security monitoring interface displaying a grid of surveillance thumbnails from a train station on Friday, December 15th. Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, begins speaking, explaining in a clear, confident voice how thousands of cameras running 24/7 generate millions of hours of footage every week, posing the challenge of making it meaningful. The interface shows filters for time, cameras, people (with color-coded clothing options), and vehicles, as Davidson emphasizes that sorting through footage to isolate one person or event could take days, but AI is able to solve this problem within seconds. The scene transitions to Davidson live on stage at Cisco Live!, standing on a dark stage illuminated by vibrant vertical neon light strips in purple, blue, red, and green. He gestures expressively to a large audience, his image projected on a large curved screen above. As he continues, the presentation returns to the Meraki Vision interface, where filters are adjusted to focus on people, including a slider for 'Object 1' from 'Rough match' to 'Exact match' and a 'Minimum event duration' slider. Davidson explains how the Meraki Vision portal can filter on objects like vehicles or people, and even go deeper with color attributes, giving the example of finding "the person in the red shirt." The video then cuts to a wide-angle surveillance view of a railway tunnel entrance with green safety mats on the tracks, as Davidson reveals that the same customer uses Meraki cameras to enable rail operations and safeguard restricted areas. He elaborates that the AI has been trained to recognize employee uniforms, so if someone enters a prohibited, designated area without the uniform, the AI will alert security. The final segment shows a split-screen view of the Meraki Vision interface in 'List View' mode, highlighting a specific event where a person in an orange jacket is seen on the train tracks, demonstrating the system’s ability to detect and flag potential safety incidents, all while the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains visible in the top right corner.

event_blocks.summary: Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, presented at Cisco Live!, highlighting Cisco's advancements in AI-powered networking and security. He began by explaining how Meraki cameras, enhanced with AI and machine learning at the edge, offer advanced security beyond facial recognition, utilizing features like automatic video encryption and authenticated firmware. Transitioning to the core topic of AI infrastructure, Davidson emphasized the power of AI computer vision integrated with the network, stating, 'Ethernet. An open Ethernet infrastructure delivers fast data transfers.' He detailed Cisco's leadership in open Ethernet, citing a Bloomberg survey where 66% of enterprises using Ethernet for AI infrastructure are Cisco customers. A significant announcement was the partnership between Cisco and NVIDIA, aimed at creating secure AI infrastructure, with Jensen Huang, Founder and CEO of NVIDIA, quoted as saying, 'Working closely with Cisco, we’re making it easier than ever for enterprises to obtain the infrastructure they need to benefit from AI.' This collaboration extends to integrating NVIDIA GPUs into Cisco servers and networking solutions. Further partnerships were highlighted, including a hyperconverged solution with Nutanix for hybrid cloud, combining compute, networking, and storage. Davidson also discussed Cisco Validated Designs, described as 'end-to-end reference architectures' that simplify the deployment of efficient AI workloads, saving time and money, and supporting platforms like Red Hat, VMware, and Nutanix. He showcased a hyperscaler's deployment using Cisco's Ethernet network fabric for AI and back-end networks, emphasizing power efficiency, cost advantage, and programmability, with a close-up of the Cisco Silicon One™ chip illustrating the hardware foundation. Davidson asserted that Cisco is 'changing the very DNA of the internet' with secure networks scaling to Zettabyte capacities and performing at terabits per second, ensuring 'five nines availability.' He cited Colt, a major European service provider, as an example, detailing how they achieved 'five nines of service availability with automation, sub-50 millisecond recovery time,' and 'up to a 97% power savings' by deploying Cisco's Silicon One and routed optical networking, with plans to calculate service power consumption and carbon footprint using Cisco's 'Acdian solution.' Davidson concluded by addressing the complexity barrier to AI adoption, which Cisco is solving with the 'Cisco Networking Cloud' and 'Cisco Security Cloud,' stating, 'Complexity is also the ally of bad actors,' and emphasizing how these solutions help mitigate the 'cycle of dread, panic, and frenzy' associated with cyber threats. The presentation featured dynamic stage lighting, large curved video screens displaying speaker images and key messages, and the consistent presence of the 'Cisco Live!' logo.

Presentation Slide.description: A slide with the text 'If it’s connected, it’s protected.' followed by two bullet points: 'Cisco Networking Cloud' and 'Cisco Security Cloud,' each with a corresponding cloud icon, displayed prominently on the curved screen.

Curved Video Screen Display.description: A large, multi-panel curved video screen above the stage that initially displays the speaker’s image and a slide about 'colt 97% power savings', then transitions to a presentation slide titled 'If it’s connected, it’s protected.' with bullet points for 'Cisco Networking Cloud' and 'Cisco Security Cloud,' visually reinforcing Jonathan Davidson's spoken points about combating complexity and bad actors.

Jonathan Davidson.description: A man with curly hair and glasses, wearing a dark blazer over a blue plaid shirt and gray trousers, speaking on stage with expressive hand gestures; identified as EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, he articulates the importance of 'service visibility and insights with microsecond granularity' for faster service monetization, and explains how 'automation, convergence, scalability, and infrastructure all come together to digitalize the entire world,' accelerated by AI. He then addresses 'complexity' as a barrier, which Cisco solves with its 'Networking Cloud' and 'Security Cloud,' vividly describing the fight against 'bad actors' and the 'dread, panic, and frenzy' of threats.

event_blocks.summary: Jeetu Patel, EVP and General Manager of Security and Collaboration at Cisco, stood on a high-tech stage at Cisco Live!, illuminated by dynamic blue and purple neon light strips, addressing an engaged audience. He announced a series of "jam-packed" and "really exciting" security-focused announcements, emphasizing security's importance for a safe world. Patel explained the evolution of the security industry, characterized by "patchwork" innovation leading to approximately "3500 or so vendors" and the challenge of managing "50 to 70 different products" within a cybersecurity stack, deeming it "frankly untenable." He highlighted a "major shift" towards integrated platforms over point solutions and introduced the "Cisco Security Cloud," an integrated platform announced "about 18 months ago." The presentation detailed the platform's focus on three key problems: user protection, cloud protection, and breach protection. For user protection, Patel illustrated the complexity of connecting to various applications, each requiring different methods, and introduced a "better way" with "STEP 1: Authenticate" to simplify user access and reduce "cognitive load." He described a secure access application integrated with ThousandEyes to manage "the plumbing behind the scenes," enabling a "zero trust, zero friction" experience by providing "least privileged access." Transitioning to cloud protection, Patel discussed extending "zero trust" principles to applications communicating in the data center and the cloud, noting that "95% of the applications that happen to be in the cloud need to access some sort of resource in your private data center." He visually demonstrated a security threat where a marketing application in AWS could access a customer database in a private data center, bypassing zero trust, and proposed a "translation layer" to enable "trusted zero-trust connection" between different environments, emphasizing Cisco's role as an "open integrated platform" that "understands both" IP and service-based communication. Patel also addressed the challenge of maintaining security policies when applications move between clouds like AWS, Azure, or GCP, before moving on to "breach protection."

Jeetu Patel.description: A bald man wearing clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, and dark trousers, holding a small device in his hand; identified as EVP and General Manager, Security and Collaboration at Cisco. He speaks clearly and articulately, explaining complex cloud security concepts, multi-cloud architecture, and breach protection, while gesturing expressively and moving confidently across the stage under dynamic blue and purple neon lighting.

It then transitions to show 'GCP' replacing 'AWS' with evolving red warning icons and dotted lines indicating a security threat, before finally displaying a 'Breach protection' slide in bold white text on a vibrant blue and green background, all in sync with Jeetu Patel's presentation.

This is followed by a new slide featuring the text 'Zero trust, zero friction' against a vibrant, multicolored background, directly aligning with the speaker's explanation of these concepts.
