Marked Stage Area.description: A square black mat with white borders on the floor, positioned in the center aisle, where the speaker pauses to address the audience, serving as a focal point for interaction. The speaker is seen standing on this area during his presentation.

raw_segments: The minute opens with Jeetu Patel, a bald man in a dark blazer over a black shirt, standing on a circular stage under a dark blue ambiance with vertical blue and purple neon light strips. He gestures expressively while holding a small device, engaging the audience as he speaks, stating that Cisco innovated more in 2023 than in the prior 10 years and predicting an "order of magnitude multiple" in 2024. He expresses pride in his team before inviting his "good friend Ronak" to discuss observability. A wide shot reveals a large, dimly lit auditorium filled with seated attendees, attentively watching a massive curved video screen above the stage. This screen initially displays Jeetu's image alongside a 'Cisco Identity Intelligence' slide detailing components like 'Smart Authentication with Cisco Duo' and 'Smart Threat Detection with Cisco XDR,' noted as 'Available July 2024.' As Jeetu concludes, the screen transitions to a vibrant rainbow-colored graphic with the text 'That’s Identity Intelligence.' Energetic background music swells as Ronak Desai, a man with graying hair and a beard, wearing a dark suit with a white shirt and a red patterned pocket square, walks confidently onto the stage. A large screen introduces him as 'Senior Vice President, General Manager, App Dynamics and Full-Stack Observability,' while a lower-third graphic also identifies him. Ronak takes the stage, holding a device, and begins his presentation with expressive hand gestures, thanking Jeetu. He then continues the "AI thread," explaining how Cisco leverages AI for full-stack observability by integrating network, security, and application intelligence to deliver a "flawless and secure digital experience for everyone." The professional, high-tech atmosphere is maintained by the consistent blue and purple neon stage lighting and the ever-present Cisco Live! logo in the top right corner.

The segment opens with a presentation slide titled 'Cisco Full-Stack Observability,' detailing 'Delivering insights, actions and outcomes,' with a diagram showing Observability at the center, connected to Networking, Security, and Applications. This transitions to a 'SHIPPING' banner-marked slide listing six 'Cisco Full-Stack Observability use cases,' including Hybrid Application Monitoring and AIOps. Ronak Desai, a man with graying hair and a beard in a dark suit with a red patterned pocket square, then appears on a circular stage in a large, dimly lit auditorium, gesturing expressively as his image is projected on massive curved screens behind him. He begins speaking, stating, "One year ago, we stood here in front of you and laid out our full-stack observability strategy." He expresses pride in his team for having "delivered every solution and use case which we talked about." The audience is visible in the foreground under blue ambient lighting, while the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains in the top right. A new slide, 'THE NEED,' appears, emphasizing 'Deliver the most optimal and secure experience for employees, partners and customers,' followed by 'Biggest Challenges' like Tool sprawl and High TCO, each with an icon. Ronak continues, explaining that "digital experience has become a key boardroom metric" and that "Cisco AppDynamics and Cisco Observability Platform is helping you achieve your observability goal." He walks across the stage, using hand gestures, as he recounts audience feedback: "you told me loud and clear that you want a unified and a simple observability experience." He further elaborates on the challenge of having "many, many monitoring tools... somewhere between 10 to 140 monitoring tools," concluding that "clearly, this is causing high total cost of ownership." The professional, high-tech atmosphere is maintained by dynamic blue stage lighting and the large screens displaying the presentation content.

On a circular stage at Cisco Live!, Ronak Desai, a man with graying hair and a beard in a dark suit, white shirt, and red patterned pocket square, gestures expressively while holding a small device. The stage is bathed in professional blue lighting with vertical neon strips, and a large curved video screen above displays his presentation. He explains how current issues are "causing frictions amongst your op teams" and "delaying your resolution and time to detect," as the screen shows a slide titled 'THE NEED' outlining 'Biggest Challenges' like Tool sprawl, High TCO, and Delayed resolution with corresponding icons. Ronak continues, stating that their "use case driven approach for full stack observability is really resonating with our customers," as the screen transitions to showcase customer testimonials from Vodafone, FAB, and easyJet. He elaborates on "unifying the data, hence reducing the frictions between your op teams, improving the operational efficiency, at the same time reducing the business risk." The camera alternates between close-ups of Ronak and wide shots of the attentive audience. The presentation then highlights Cisco's recognition as a 'Gartner Peer Insights Customers’ Choice 2023' for Application Performance Monitoring & Observability, with Ronak affirming, "Gartner agrees with us. They've recognized us as Customers' Choice, Peer Inside for Observability in 2023." The final slide displayed lists 'Top 4 Cisco Full-Stack Observability use cases,' including SAP Monitoring and AIOps, as Ronak mentions, "We talked about a lot of use cases," and expresses his "pure joy and opportunity for you all to see that live on the show floor." The Cisco Live! logo remains visible in the top right corner throughout the segment.

The video opens with a presentation slide titled 'SAP monitoring and troubleshooting with Cisco AppDynamics,' marked with a 'SHIPPING' banner, listing three key use cases. A male speaker, Ronak Desai, a man with graying hair and a beard in a dark suit, white shirt, and red patterned pocket square, begins his presentation, stating, "But here are the four use cases which I really want to kind of spend time today. The first one is around SAP monitoring." The scene transitions to a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium with blue ambient lighting, where Ronak stands on a circular white stage, gesturing expressively with a small device in hand. He addresses the attentively watching audience, explaining that "SAP users really want to understand bottleneck into their SAP transactions," aiming for predictive and proactive root cause analysis to avoid outages. He emphasizes the financial impact, stating, "You know one minute of outage on SAP landscape can cost you tens of thousands of dollars," and offers, "So let us help you reduce that pain." As Ronak continues to engage the audience with hand gestures, moving across the stage and pausing on a marked square black mat, the large curved video screen above him transitions from the SAP monitoring slide to a new one titled 'Business Risk Observability,' featuring a green 'ANNOUNCING' banner and bullet points for 'Data Security Posture Management (DSPM)' and 'Vulnerability and Threat Intelligence.' He introduces this, saying, "The second use case is around business risk observability. You know security risk assessments are top of the mind for our security ops, IT ops, and application team. And I'm very happy to announce extension..." The stage is illuminated by vertical neon light strips in blue and purple, creating a high-tech atmosphere, with the Cisco Live! logo consistently visible in the top right corner.

The minute begins with a presentation slide titled 'Business Risk Observability,' prominently announcing 'Data Security Posture Management (DSPM)' with a green banner, while the speaker, Ronak Desai, clearly and confidently explains how this "data security posture management observability" helps "detect, discover, analyze, and quickly take action to protect your data and protect your brand." The slide visually supports his points with a dashboard interface for 'Snowflake_US_East,' displaying database attributes and a table of alerts by severity, alongside bullet points detailing GenAI-powered data security and compliance. As Ronak transitions to "The third use case is around digital experience monitoring," the scene shifts to a 'Cisco Digital Experience Monitoring' slide, marked with a yellow 'SHIPPING' banner, illustrating a flow from various digital touchpoints to Cisco's monitoring solution, promising "Minutes to triage and recover" and featuring a testimonial from First Abu Dhabi Bank. A wide shot then reveals Ronak Desai, a man with graying hair and a beard, in a dark suit, gesturing expressively on a circular stage illuminated with blue lighting, addressing an attentive audience seated in rows, with the presentation slides displayed on large curved screens above. He continues, emphasizing the need to understand "every component your application depends upon" to deliver a "flawless digital experience," highlighting Cisco's "unique advantage" in providing "end-to-end holistic understanding" by "ingesting network insight from ThousandEyes and private insight from AppDynamics." The view returns to the 'Cisco Digital Experience Monitoring' slide before transitioning to a new slide announcing 'Cisco Digital Experience Monitoring with Session Replay' and 'Browser Real User Monitoring (BRUM) and Mobile Real User Monitoring (MRUM),' showcasing performance graphs for 'cloudkart-public' as Ronak concludes, "And I am very happy to..." The 'Cisco Live!' logo remains consistently visible in the top right corner throughout the segment.

event_blocks.summary: On a circular stage bathed in blue light, Jeetu Patel, dressed in a dark blazer over a black shirt, presented 'Cisco Digital Experience Monitoring with Session Replay' and 'Browser Real User Monitoring (BRUM) and Mobile Real User Monitoring (MRUM)' from a presentation slide featuring a dashboard with 'Requests per Minute' and 'End User Response Time Distribution' graphs. Holding a small device and gesturing, Patel announced the GA launch of session replay, comparing it to Netflix and emphasizing its ability to simplify analytics for identifying problematic user sessions. As the camera pulled back to reveal the large, curved video screen displaying the slide and the dimly lit auditorium, he encouraged audience members to experience the demo on the floor, acknowledging the immense pressure on IT teams. The presentation then transitioned to a 'Cisco AIOps' slide, highlighting 'Unified monitoring', 'Real-time business health', and 'Cross-domain service insights' with a dashboard showing 'Incident Count By Priority' and 100% 'Availability'. Returning to a medium shot, Patel announced the live launch of Cisco AIOps, promising actionable insights to improve IT operations and reduce mean time to detect and repair, explaining its role in streamlining incident and change management through correlated faults. The 'Cisco Live!' logo remained visible in the top right corner throughout.

Presentation Slide.description: A series of slides with titles 'Cisco Digital Experience Monitoring' and 'Cisco AIOps', featuring detailed dashboards and graphs. The 'Cisco Digital Experience Monitoring' slide includes a green 'ANNOUNCING' banner and performance graphs for 'cloudkart-public', aligning with Jeetu Patel's verbal announcement of its extension and GA release. The 'Cisco AIOps' slide includes a green 'ANNOUNCING' banner, a dashboard for 'Organization: Demo IT' with 'Incident Count By Priority' and 'Availability' at 100%, and bullet points for 'Unified monitoring', 'Real-time business health', and 'Cross-domain service insights', directly corresponding to Jeetu's announcement of its launch and features.

It shows the 'Cisco Digital Experience Monitoring' slide with a 'SHIPPING' banner and performance graphs, then transitions to the 'Cisco AIOps' slide with a 'ANNOUNCING' banner and a dashboard for 'Organization: Demo IT', visually reinforcing the product announcements made by Jeetu Patel.

Cisco Live!.description: The event branding visible in the top right corner of the screen, with a stylized logo and consistent presence throughout the segment, reinforcing the event's identity. It remains static and unchanging during the entire minute.

He holds a small device in his right hand and gestures expressively with his left hand while speaking in a clear, confident, and engaging tone, announcing new Cisco features and explaining their benefits. He is shown in both medium and wide shots, actively engaging with the audience and the presentation content.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a presentation slide announcing 'Cisco Digital Experience Monitoring with Session Replay' and 'Browser Real User Monitoring (BRUM) and Mobile Real User Monitoring (MRUM)', featuring a dashboard with graphs for 'Requests per Minute' and 'End User Response Time Distribution'. Jeetu Patel, a bald man with clear-framed glasses, wearing a dark blazer over a black shirt and dark trousers, stands on a circular stage, holding a small device and gesturing expressively. He confidently announces, "we are extending the digital experience monitoring by adding session replay as part of that. And it is going GA this week. It's like Netflix." As he speaks about accessing millions of users and simplifying analytics to quickly narrow down problematic sessions, the camera pulls back to a wide shot, revealing the large, dimly lit auditorium with blue stage lighting and a massive curved screen displaying the presentation slide. Jeetu walks across the stage, engaging with the audience, emphasizing the ease of use: "And it is that simple. Please do go experience that for yourself on the demo floor." He continues, acknowledging the high pressure on IT teams, "pressure on you and your IT team is higher than ever." The presentation then shifts to a new slide announcing 'Cisco AIOps', highlighting 'Unified monitoring', 'Real-time business health', and 'Cross-domain service insights', with a dashboard showing 'Incident Count By Priority' and 'Availability' at 100%. Returning to a medium shot, Jeetu continues to speak and gesture, announcing, "So this week, Cisco AIOps is going live. This is providing you actionable insights to help you improve your IT operation, reducing your mean time to detect and repair." He further explains how it helps streamline incident and change management by focusing on correlated faults and insights. The 'Cisco Live!' logo remains consistently visible in the top right corner throughout the segment.

event_blocks.summary: Ronak Desai, dressed in a dark suit with a red pocket square, addressed the audience at Cisco Live! from a dimly lit stage, gesturing expressively while holding a device and stating, "You ask for it and we are delivering it." The scene revealed Ronak on a circular platform before a large, dark audience bathed in blue ambient lighting, with vertical blue and purple neon light strips enhancing the high-tech atmosphere. A wide-angle view showcased the massive curved video screen displaying Ronak's image and a presentation slide titled 'GenAI in Cisco AppDynamics and Full-Stack Observability.' Ronak explained the availability of monitoring LLM usage from cost and API perspectives, aligning with the 'SHIPPING' phase on the slide, and announced the upcoming addition of a natural language interface for interacting with their tools. He further revealed plans to integrate autonomous root cause analysis with Cisco AI Assistant for Observability. The presentation shifted to a slide titled 'Platform apps & modules, Partner Extensibility' under 'Cisco Observability Platform,' where Ronak highlighted the platform's open, API-driven, and extensible nature, enabling rapid innovation for partners. He concluded by happily announcing the launch of eight new partner modules, beginning with business insight and ML, as the 'Cisco Live!' logo remained visible on the screen, contributing to a professional and immersive tech-focused ambiance.

Ronak Desai.description: A man with graying hair and a beard, wearing a dark suit with a white shirt and a red patterned pocket square, standing on a circular stage and gesturing expressively while presenting. He holds a small device in his hand and is shown in both wide and medium shots, actively engaging with the audience and the presentation content. He speaks clearly and confidently, announcing key product developments such as the availability of LLM usage monitoring, the addition of a natural language interface for query language, autonomous root cause analysis with Cisco AI Assistant for Observability, and the launch of eight new partner modules for the Cisco Observability Platform, highlighting its open, API-driven, and extensible nature.

raw_segments: The segment opens with Ronak Desai, a man with graying hair and a beard, wearing a dark suit, white shirt, and red patterned pocket square, speaking expressively on a dimly lit stage. He gestures with his right hand while holding a small device in his left, declaring, "You ask for it and we are delivering it." The camera pulls back to reveal Ronak on a circular platform before a large, dark audience illuminated by blue ambient lighting and flanked by vertical blue and purple neon light strips, creating a high-tech atmosphere. As he continues, the camera cuts to a wide-angle view showing the massive curved video screen above the stage, displaying his image and a presentation slide titled 'GenAI in Cisco AppDynamics and Full-Stack Observability.' Ronak explains that "Monitoring LLM usage by your applications from a cost perspective and an API perspective is already available," aligning with the 'SHIPPING' phase on the slide. The presentation transitions to another slide, 'Platform apps & modules, Partner Extensibility' under 'Cisco Observability Platform,' as Ronak emphasizes the platform's uniqueness, stating it's "open, its API driven, and it's extensible," and that this extensibility enables "rapid innovation on this platform" for partners. He concludes by happily announcing "eight new partner modules starting from business insight, ML..." while gesturing towards the screen, with the 'Cisco Live!' logo consistently visible in the top right corner, maintaining a professional, immersive, and tech-focused ambiance.

event_blocks.summary: Jeetu Patel, wearing clear-framed glasses and a dark blazer, stood on a circular stage, holding a device and gesturing while explaining the rapid innovation pace enabled by the platform, noting that partners delivered 24 modules since the platform went General Availability. Behind him, a presentation slide displayed 'Platform apps & modules, Partner Extensibility' under the 'Cisco Observability Platform' heading, listing partner integrations and the 'Cisco Live!' logo. The camera then widened to show the auditorium and a large curved video screen displaying Petek Ergul, Managing Director at HSBC. Jeetu introduced Petek, highlighting her 'great vision for full stack observability,' and she walked confidently from the audience area onto the stage. Enthusiastic applause and cheering from the audience, accompanied by upbeat music, marked her arrival as she met Jeetu center stage and they shook hands. Petek, wearing dark-framed glasses and a geometric necklace, stood beside Jeetu, thanked him for the invitation, and then responded to his prompt about her CEO's recent public statement.

event_blocks.summary: The Cisco Live! event features Petek Ergul, Managing Director and Global Head of CTO Service Management at HSBC, on a circular stage bathed in blue spotlights, with her image and details displayed on a massive curved video screen. Initially in a close-up, the camera pulls back to reveal the dark auditorium setting. Petek, dressed in a black blazer with silver embellishments and a distinctive geometric pendant, walks onto the stage and is introduced by Raunak, who highlights HSBC's investment in digitization as both an opportunity and a challenge. Petek articulates HSBC's digital transformation, aiming to put the "full power of our bank in every customer's pocket" through easier, more secure digital banking, and details their long-term technology strategy, "Vision 27," which involves demising legacy applications to streamline their complex technology architecture. She further explains the increasing complexity and number of changes delivered into their production network, noting over 800,000 changes were delivered last year, a 19% increase, with expectations to exceed 1 million this year. Petek emphasizes the need for a sharp focus on protecting production by uplifting technology resilience, investing in observability and AIOps, which is punctuated by a "boing" sound effect. Jeetu Patel, standing attentively to her right, clarifies, "That's not me," in response to being addressed. The stage is a circular platform with vertical blue light strips, and the 'Cisco Live!' logo is consistently visible.

The camera then pulls back to a wide shot, revealing a large, dark auditorium with a circular stage bathed in blue spotlights and a massive curved video screen above, displaying Petek Ergul’s image, name, title ('Managing Dir, Global Head of CTO Service Management, HSBC'), and company logo. Petek walks confidently from the audience area onto the stage, where she shakes hands with Raunak, a bald man with clear-framed glasses, wearing a dark blazer over a black shirt, who introduces HSBC's strategy to invest in digitization and technology as a "huge opportunity and also a big challenge," asking Petek how it represents for her and her teams. The video opens with a close-up of Petek Ergul, Managing Director, Global Head of CTO Service Management at HSBC, standing on a dark stage with a deep blue backdrop. She has a blonde bob, wears black-rimmed glasses with white temples, a black blazer with silver embellishments, a black top with a white abstract pattern, and a distinctive geometric pendant necklace. Speaking animatedly with head turns and hand gestures, Petek articulates in a clear, articulate voice with a slight accent, "Now, digitize at scale with growing dependency on third-party services means increasing complexity in our environments. The camera alternates between close-ups and medium shots, capturing her expressive delivery. Petek addresses "Ronak" and emphasizes HSBC's transformation into a digital bank, stating, "we at tech in technology, what we must really do is have a very sharp focus in protecting our production. And that is only possible by uplifting our technology resilience, which also includes investing in observability and AIOps." A distinct "boing" sound effect punctuates her final words. event_blocks.summary: At Cisco Live!, within a large, dimly lit auditorium featuring a circular stage illuminated by spotlights and branded with the 'Cisco Live!' logo, Petek Ergul, Managing Director, Global Head of CTO Service Management at HSBC, and Ronak Desai, a man in a dark suit, engaged in a discussion about technology resilience. Ronak emphasized that 'security and uptime... is not negotiable' and queried Petek on HSBC's approach to technology resilience, specifically concerning observability. Petek, dressed in a black blazer with silver embellishments and a black top with a white abstract pattern, affirmed resilience's non-negotiable status, explaining that HSBC's 'license to operate very much rests on trust' with customers, staff, and regulators. She detailed HSBC's Vision 2027 strategy, which includes a unique self-assessment tool called the Technology Resilience Maturity Framework (TRMF). Later, Petek, with animated hand gestures and her image displayed on large screens, elaborated on TRMF's function in assessing resilience maturity across technology stacks and with third-party partners, identifying uplift requirements and providing a common language for investment prioritization. Jeetu Patel, in a dark suit and red shoes, listened attentively, occasionally nodding. Petek highlighted observability as crucial for identifying anomalies and proactively preventing incidents, enabling faster detection and restoration, and providing insights for root cause analysis. She further explained that observability aligns technology teams with customer outcomes and should be tied to service level objectives, noting significant progress in monitoring, alerting, and logging capabilities. The stage was dynamically lit with blue and purple neon strips, creating a modern, tech-focused ambiance.

Petek Ergul.description: A woman with a blonde bob, black-rimmed glasses with white temples, a black blazer with silver embellishments, a black top with a white abstract pattern, and a distinctive geometric pendant necklace, speaking passionately and articulately on stage at Cisco Live! as Managing Director, Global Head of CTO Service Management at HSBC, discussing identifying faults, preventing incidents, and ensuring technology teams are aligned for customer outcomes through observability and improved monitoring capabilities.

Ronak Desai.description: A man with graying hair and a beard, wearing a dark suit with a white shirt and a red patterned pocket square, standing on a circular stage and gesturing expressively while presenting, holding a small device in his hand, whose clear voice asks about the bank's role, the non-negotiable nature of security and uptime, and specifically questions Petek about HSBC's approach to technology resilience in the context of observability.

He engages with Petek Ergul, asking a clear and direct question about the meaning of a consistent and unified approach for HSBC, its teams, and customers, accompanied by expressive hand gestures.

raw_segments: The video opens with a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium at Cisco Live!, featuring a circular stage illuminated by spotlights and surrounded by a vast audience. On stage, Petek Ergul, a woman with a blonde bob, black-rimmed glasses with white temples, a black blazer with silver embellishments, a black top with a white abstract pattern, and a distinctive geometric pendant necklace, stands facing Ronak Desai, a man in a dark suit with a white shirt and red patterned pocket square, holding a small device. Ronak gestures expressively as he speaks, his clear voice discussing the bank's role in the global economy and asserting that "security and uptime, I'm sure, is not negotiable." He then directly asks Petek, "So what does technology resilience mean to HSBC? How do you approach it specifically in context of observability?" Petek listens attentively, and the camera cuts to a close-up of her as she begins to speak, her expression animated and her hands gesturing to emphasize her points, her image simultaneously displayed on the large screens behind the stage. With a clear and articulate voice, Petek affirms, "You're absolutely spot on, resilience is not negotiable." She explains that as a bank, HSBC's "license to operate very much rests on trust," encompassing customers, staff, regulators, and the public, assuring the audience, "we take this responsibility very, very seriously," which is why they adopt "a very proactive approach to technology resilience." She continues, detailing that as part of HSBC's Vision 2027 technology strategy, they "developed a very powerful and very unique tool for our industry," which is a "self-assessment tool called Technology Resilience Maturity Framework," or TRMF. The camera alternates between close-ups of Petek and wider shots showing both speakers on the stage, capturing their interaction, all while the professional and engaging atmosphere, the audience in darkness, the 'Cisco Live!' logo, and the blue and white lighting maintain a modern, tech-focused ambiance.

On a circular stage at Cisco Live!, Petek Ergul, dressed in a black blazer with silver embellishments and a black top with a white abstract pattern, gestures expressively as she speaks about the 'TRMF' (Technology Resilience Maturity Framework). Her clear, professional voice explains how TRMF is used to assess and categorize resilience maturity levels across technology stacks and with third-party partners, determining uplift requirements. Jeetu Patel, in a dark suit with a white shirt and red shoes, listens attentively, holding notes and a small device, occasionally nodding in response. Petek emphasizes that TRMF provides a common language between business and technology colleagues, enabling them to make priority calls for investments to uplift resilience. As she continues, walking slightly across the stage, she details that TRMF includes various criteria, notably observability, which she highlights as crucial for identifying anomalies, referencing a point Jeetu had made earlier. The stage is illuminated by blue vertical light strips, with the audience visible in the dimly lit auditorium, while the Cisco Live! logo remains visible in the top right corner, reinforcing the event's branding.

The segment opens with a close-up of Petek Ergul, Managing Director, Global Head of CTO Service Management at HSBC, speaking passionately on stage at Cisco Live!. Dressed in a black blazer with silver embellishments, a black top with a white abstract pattern, and distinctive black-rimmed glasses with white temples, she gestures expressively with her hands. She articulates how their approach helps "identify, probably previously unknown faults or problem areas," allowing them to "proactively prevent incidents" and "detect and restore instance much faster," while also providing "insights to much better, more accurate ways to identify root causes for any incidents." The camera pulls back to reveal Jeetu Patel, a bald man in a dark blazer, standing opposite her, holding notes and a small device. He responds with animated hand gestures, asking, "So in business terms for HSBC, what does this consistent and unified approach to absolutely mean to you, your teams and to all our customers here?" Petek then replies, "Yes. So first of all, observability must ensure the technology teams are more aligned and more focused on customer outcomes." She continues, explaining it "should really closely tied into the service defined service level objectives," and that "over many years, we have made significant progress uplifting our monitoring, alerting and logging capabilities by introduction of..." The stage is dynamically lit with vertical blue and purple neon strips, and the 'Cisco Live!' logo is visible in the dark background, with an attentive audience in the foreground.

event_blocks.summary: At Cisco Live!, Petek Ergul, Managing Director, Global Head of CTO Service Management at HSBC, wearing a black blazer with silver embellishments and a geometric pendant, passionately explained on stage how tools like AppDynamics, ThousandEyes, and Splunk facilitate dealing with environmental complexity, stressing the objective of achieving "end-to-end, unified observability" across all IT service chain components for enhanced business service health visibility and global consistency. The camera captured a wide shot of the blue-lit circular stage, showing Petek on the left and Ronak Desai, in a dark suit, on the right, before a large audience. Petek elaborated on integrating observability with their TRMF tool for a live view of their resiliency posture and strengthened risk management. Ronak then took the microphone, thanking Petek and announcing an "I talk" with her at noon, gesturing to a promotional slide that read '...But wait, there’s more!' and invited attendees to an 'Innovation Talk' today from 12:00 PM to 12:30 PM CET in the 'Innovation Theater' to explore HSBC’s Cisco AppDynamics and FSO journey. He also highlighted a QR code on the slide for 'Observability at Cisco Live EMEA', with the 'Cisco Live!' logo visible.

Promotional Slide.description: A large screen display with a blue and white design, featuring the text '...But wait, there’s more!', an invitation to join Ronak and Petek for a deeper look at HSBC’s Cisco AppDynamics and FSO journey, details for an 'Innovation Talk' (INTAPP-1200) scheduled for 'TODAY 12:00 PM – 12:30 PM CET' in the 'Innovation Theater', and a QR code for 'Observability at Cisco Live EMEA'. Ronak refers to the QR code on this slide for announcements.

He thanks Petek, mentions an upcoming 'I talk' with her at noon, and gestures toward a promotional slide, directing attention to a QR code for announcements.

She speaks with a clear, articulate, and professional voice about leveraging tools like AppDynamics, ThousandEyes, and Splunk to achieve end-to-end, unified observability across IT service chains, aiming for better visibility into business service health, and integrating observability capabilities for enhanced resiliency and risk management.

event_blocks.summary: The Innovation Theater at Cisco Live! was set for an 'Innovation Talk' featuring Ronak and Petek, promoting 'Delivering and Securing Exceptional Digital Experiences for Your Business' with a QR code for 'Observability at Cisco Live EMEA.' As upbeat music and applause faded, Jeetu Patel, dressed in a dark blazer and clear-framed glasses, confidently walked onto the circular stage, holding a small device and wearing a headset microphone. He immediately addressed the audience, stating, "Alright, you thought you'd get rid of me, you're not getting rid of me that easy." Patel announced two key board initiatives: "hybrid work" and "customer experience," emphasizing the need for enhanced brand loyalty. The large Curved Video Screen Display behind him initially showed slides related to 'HYBRID WORK' and 'Reimagining workspaces,' then transitioned to 'Distance Zero: Making separation a thing of the past,' illustrating modern conference rooms. Patel elaborated on "reimagining workspaces," stressing that "people work from everywhere" and the necessity of rethinking physical space utilization, with the screen displaying a vibrant gradient background. He further explained that "98% of meetings that you'll see in the future will have at least one participant that's going to be not in the same physical location as everyone else," and noted that "only a third of the conference rooms in the world are actually video enabled." Patel introduced "distance zero" as a core principle for "innovating in this new world of hybrid work," clarifying it's about removing distance between parties not in the same physical space, not merely adding video. He detailed providing "blueprints for every different kind of room" to customers, ensuring immersion regardless of location. The screen then showcased 'Distance Zero in any meeting, in any room,' featuring diverse meeting setups with Cisco collaboration technology. Patel highlighted a "complete portfolio of devices" for "every single space," including the Desk Pro, Board Pro 55/75, Room Kit EQX, Room Bar, Room Bar Pro, Room Kit EQ, Room Kit Pro, Room Navigator, Desk Camera 4K, Headset 320 Series, and Headset 720 Series, many bearing a 'Certified for Microsoft Teams' badge. He revealed a strategic shift from devices working exclusively on the "Webex platform" to supporting "any platform that customers are using," specifically mentioning compatibility with "Zoom" and "Google," and a "special partnership with Microsoft" for native Microsoft Teams Rooms. Patel also disclosed a decision made "five or six years ago" to equip "every one of our devices" with an Nvidia chipset for AI capabilities, which is now "really proving out to be extremely valuable," fundamentally changing their mindset about device innovation.

Curved Video Screen Display.description: A large, multi-panel curved video screen above the stage that displays a presentation slide titled 'A complete portfolio of devices.' This slide visually supports Jeetu Patel's discussion of Cisco's comprehensive product range and their multi-platform strategy, featuring a horizontal lineup of Cisco collaboration products (Desk Pro, Board Pro 55/75, Room Kit EQX, Room Bar, Room Bar Pro, Room Kit EQ, Room Kit Pro, Room Navigator, Desk Camera 4K, Headset 320 Series, and Headset 720 Series), with release or certification dates and a 'Certified for Microsoft Teams' badge, directly illustrating the devices he refers to as being open to various platforms and incorporating AI capabilities.

It remains static and unchanging during the entire minute, serving as the backdrop for Jeetu Patel's announcements.

event_blocks.summary: At Cisco Live!, Jeetu Patel, dressed in a dark blazer over a black shirt and dark trousers, stood on a circular white stage, gesturing expressively into a headset microphone. He explained that Cisco's AI capabilities enhance integrated platforms, benefiting applications like Microsoft Teams with noise removal and video intelligence baked into devices. The camera pulled back to reveal a wide shot of the auditorium, showcasing large screens displaying a slide titled 'A complete portfolio of devices,' listing Cisco collaboration products. Jeetu emphasized Cisco's comprehensive portfolio beyond just collaboration and hybrid work. The presentation shifted to a slide titled 'Deliver workspace optimization and sustainability with Cisco Spaces,' featuring a 3D floor plan of Meraki San Francisco SFO12 with real-time occupancy, air quality, and desk usage data, derived from Meraki Wi-Fi access points and IoT devices, which Jeetu described as 'reimagining workspaces themselves.' Later, he highlighted the 'Webex Suite,' detailing its features like Meetings, Messaging, Calling, Webinars, Events, Whiteboarding, Polling, and Async Video, noting its affordability and comprehensive security. He also discussed the 'Apple + Cisco Better together' initiative, emphasizing their collaboration on network integration and mobile devices, and building anticipation for the launch of the Apple Vision Pro. Throughout the event, the 'Cisco Live!' logo was consistently visible, and the stage was illuminated by dynamic blue and purple lighting, with the audience bathed in ambient blue light.

He holds a small device and gestures expressively into a headset microphone, explaining that Cisco's "AI capabilities are not just in our devices, but those capabilities for AI can make every other platform that integrates with our devices better." He specifically notes that "if you actually use Microsoft Teams, they will get benefit of noise removal and noise cancellation and video intelligence, because that's something that's baked into the devices." The camera then pulls back to reveal a wide shot of the large auditorium, showing the expansive stage with multiple large screens displaying a slide titled 'A complete portfolio of devices,' listing Cisco collaboration products like Desk Pro, Board Pro 55/75, Room Kit EQX, and Headset 720 Series, along with certification dates and a 'Certified for Microsoft Teams' badge. The presentation transitions to a new slide titled 'Deliver workspace optimization and sustainability with Cisco Spaces,' which displays a detailed 3D floor plan of the Meraki San Francisco SFO12 5th Floor, showing real-time data on occupancy, indoor air quality, and desk usage. Jeetu explains that this capability allows them "to deliver workspace optimization through Cisco Spaces," taking "telemetry from Meraki Wi-Fi access points and IoT devices" to provide "a schematic of exactly what your space looks like, what spaces are available," and also taking "the telemetry from the devices." He concludes by stating, "that's reimagining workspaces themselves." Throughout, the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains consistently visible in the top right corner, and the dynamic blue lighting illuminates the stage and audience.

He holds a small black device and gestures expressively into a headset microphone, articulating Cisco's core principles of a frictionless experience, honoring privacy, and keeping organizations safe.
