event_blocks.summary: At Cisco Live!, Jeetu Patel, wearing a dark blazer, black shirt, and clear-framed glasses, stood on a circular white stage under dynamic blue stage lighting, addressing a packed audience. A voiceover introduced "Webex for Apple Vision Pro," showcasing a spatial computing demonstration with floating app icons and multiple participants in video windows, highlighting features like real-time translation and AI noise removal, and stating, "where every space is your favorite workspace" with an "infinite canvas." Patel, holding a small black device and speaking into a headset microphone, announced its availability in the App Store, met with applause, while the curved video screen displayed 'Apple + Cisco Better together.' He emphasized Cisco's commitment to the Apple partnership, focusing on privacy and security. The demonstration then highlighted seamless integration, concluding with the Webex by Cisco logo. Patel further discussed reimagining workspaces and customer experience, transitioning the screen to a 'Delivering great customer experiences' slide detailing 'Proactive communications,' 'Self-service,' and 'Human engagement' connected by the 'Webex Platform.' He explained leveraging the Webex platform for world-class customer experience applications, enabling proactive communication and self-service across multiple channels like chat, voice, WhatsApp, and video, maintaining context. The stage lighting featured blue and green vertical light strips, with the Cisco Live! logo visible. Patel announced "solutions for everyone," including a "contact center basic" solution included in the Webex Suite for smaller teams, and an "Essentials version" for $30 per user per month, designed for customer-facing teams of any size, empowering individuals to help solve customer problems. The screen visually reinforced these tiers, with the 'Basic' tier labeled 'INCLUDED in Webex Suite' and the 'Essentials' tier showing '$30 USD per user per month' for 'Customer-facing teams (any size).'

Curved Video Screen Display.description: A large, multi-panel curved video screen above the stage that displays the speaker’s image and presentation slides. In this segment, it shows the 'Solutions for everyone' slide with three tiers, emphasizing the scalability of the offerings for different team sizes and budgets.

Cisco Live!.description: The event branding visible in the top right corner of the screen, with a stylized logo and consistent presence throughout the segment, reinforcing the event's identity. It remains static and unchanging during the entire minute.

Webex by Cisco.description: The brand logo displayed prominently in the final frames, featuring a green and blue 'W' symbol above the text 'webex by cisco' on a vibrant purple background, reinforcing the product identity.

Stage Lighting Setup.description: Dynamic lighting with blue and green hues, including spotlights and ambient stage lighting, creating a professional and immersive environment. The lighting accentuates the speaker and the stage, contributing to the professional atmosphere.

Webex for Apple Vision Pro.description: A digital demonstration showcasing a futuristic living room environment with floating app icons like Apple TV, Music, and Webex. The Webex interface is displayed in a spatial computing context, with multiple participants appearing in floating video windows, engaging in a collaborative meeting. Features such as real-time closed captions (with translation from English to Spanish), interactive whiteboarding, and live polling via Slido are demonstrated, emphasizing immersive, multi-user collaboration, while a female voiceover describes it as entering 'the era of spatial computing' where 'every space is your favorite workspace' with an 'infinite canvas' and 'industry-leading features like real-time translation and AI noise removal'.

Jeetu Patel.description: A bald man with clear-framed glasses, wearing a dark blazer over a black shirt and dark trousers, standing on a circular white stage at Cisco Live!. He holds a small black device in one hand and gestures expressively with the other while speaking into a headset microphone, moving dynamically across the stage and engaging the audience with animated hand movements and direct eye contact. He speaks with a clear, engaging voice, announcing new contact center solutions and their pricing tiers, specifically detailing the 'Basic' and 'Essentials' versions.

Audience Members.description: A diverse group of attendees seated in rows, many wearing lanyards with badges, dressed in business casual to formal attire, actively watching and reacting to the speaker. The audience is visible in wide shots, indicating high engagement with the presentation.

Presentation Slide.description: A slide displayed on the curved screen featuring the text 'Delivering great customer experiences' with three components: 'Proactive communications', 'Self-service', and 'Human engagement', each with corresponding icons (e.g., WhatsApp, Facebook, email, chatbots, Instagram, Messenger, and a headset). Below, the 'Webex Platform' is shown with a layered icon, visually connecting the three components, which Jeetu Patel elaborates on as he discusses leveraging the Webex platform for world-class customer experience, proactive communications, and self-service.

Innovation Theater.description: A large, dimly lit auditorium with rows of seated attendees and multiple large screens displaying presentation content, with blue and green lighting accents. The stage is circular and elevated, with the speaker positioned centrally, and the audience is visible in wide shots, indicating high engagement with the presentation.

Marked Stage Area.description: A square black mat with white borders on the floor, positioned in the center aisle, where the speaker pauses to address the audience, serving as a focal point for interaction. The speaker is seen standing on this area during his presentation.

Solutions for everyone.description: A presentation slide displayed on the curved screen, featuring three tiers: 'Basic' (Smaller, tactical teams, INCLUDED in Webex Suite), 'Essentials' (Customer-facing teams (any size), 30 USD per user per month), and 'Standard | Premium' (Feature-rich contact center). The slide uses a blue and white color scheme with abstract wave designs in the background, and its content is directly referenced and explained by the speaker, detailing the features and pricing of the 'Basic' and 'Essentials' tiers.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium at Cisco Live!, where Jeetu Patel stands on a circular white stage under dynamic blue stage lighting, addressing a packed audience. A female voiceover begins, announcing, "Enter the era of spatial computing, Webex for Apple Vision Pro." This accompanies a digital demonstration of Webex for Apple Vision Pro, showcasing a futuristic living room environment with floating app icons like Apple TV, Music, and Webex. The Webex interface is displayed in a spatial computing context, with multiple participants appearing in floating video windows, engaging in a collaborative meeting. The voiceover continues, describing it as a space "where every space is your favorite workspace" with an "infinite canvas" for collaboration, leveraging "industry-leading features like real-time translation and AI noise removal." The scene then cuts back to Jeetu Patel on stage, wearing a dark blazer, black shirt, and clear-framed glasses, holding a small black device and gesturing expressively while speaking into a headset microphone. He announces, "Available in the App Store today," which is met with enthusiastic applause from the attentive audience. The large curved video screen behind him displays 'Apple + Cisco Better together'. Jeetu continues, expressing excitement about the Apple partnership and emphasizing Cisco's commitment to investing in the partnership and building amazing products with privacy and security at the forefront. The segment concludes with a return to the Webex demonstration, highlighting seamless integration, followed by a final screen displaying the Webex by Cisco logo on a vibrant purple background.

The video opens in a dimly lit Cisco Live! auditorium, where Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses and dark attire, stands on a circular white stage under a massive curved video screen initially displaying 'Apple + Cisco Better together'. The audience is bathed in blue ambient lighting. Jeetu, holding a small black device and gesturing expressively with a headset microphone, walks dynamically across the stage, engaging the crowd. He speaks about being "completely immersed into it," stating, "that's Apple," before posing the question, "How do you also make sure that you don't just reimagine workspaces and work, but also customer experience?" As he continues, the screen transitions to a slide titled 'Delivering great customer experiences', detailing 'Proactive communications', 'Self-service', and 'Human engagement' connected by the 'Webex Platform'. Jeetu emphasizes leveraging "that same platform that we've used for video and audio, um, for Webex," to build "world-class customer experience set of applications." He explains how the core Webex platform enables not just reactive customer service but also "proactive communications," where "the customer shouldn't even have to call because something's gone wrong," and the brand can "proactively make sure that you take care of that customer." He adds the importance of providing "every customer the ability to self-serve." The camera alternates between close-ups of Jeetu and wide shots of the stage and engaged audience, highlighting the scale of the event, all while the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains visible in the top right corner amidst dynamic blue and green neon lighting.

The video opens with a close-up of Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer, black shirt, and dark trousers, standing on a circular white stage at Cisco Live!. He holds a small black device and gestures expressively with his free hand, speaking into a headset microphone. His clear, confident voice explains, "there is such a thing as wanting to have the desire to talk to a human from time to time," emphasizing that "we want to make sure that you don't have to keep repeating yourself, you don't have to keep feeling frustrated, and you can actually talk to the human and get your problem solved as easily as possible." As he walks across the stage, the camera pulls back to reveal the full auditorium with seated attendees and a large curved screen above, displaying a 'Webex Platform' slide with 'Proactive communications', 'Self-service', and 'Human engagement'. Jeetu continues, "you might start that communication with that brand on a website, in chat, and then you might decide to jump on a voice call," highlighting that "we will actually keep complete context of that, and all of those multiple channels, whether it be WhatsApp or websites or Instagram or a telephone call or a video call, will all actually maintain context." He points towards the screen and audience with dynamic hand movements, reinforcing his points about not having to repeat oneself. The stage, illuminated with blue and green vertical light strips, and the minimalist design keep the focus on Jeetu, who concludes, "we've actually made these solutions available for everyone," mentioning a past "standard and premium version which was the feature-rich contact center." The Cisco Live! logo remains visible, and the attentive audience watches as Jeetu delivers his energetic and informative presentation.

Jeetu Patel, a bald man in a dark blazer, stands on a circular white stage at Cisco Live!, holding a small black device and gesturing expressively into a headset microphone. He announces a "contact center basic" solution, explaining in a clear, engaging voice that it's designed for "smaller, more tactical teams that might not need a very heavy-duty contact center," and highlights that "that's included in the Webex Suite." Behind him, a massive curved video screen displays a 'Solutions for everyone' slide, visually reinforcing his words with the 'Basic' tier labeled 'INCLUDED in Webex Suite' for 'Smaller, tactical teams.' He elaborates that purchasing the Webex Suite provides this basic contact center, offering multi-line communication and features beneficial for IT help desks or HR ticketing systems. As he moves dynamically across the stage, engaging the audience with animated hand movements, he then declares, "And so what we've done is we've said, let's take it a step further." He proceeds to announce "the Essentials version," which he states will be available for "a much lower price than the full-fledged standard version for $30 per user per month." The 'Essentials' tier on the screen aligns perfectly, showing 'Customer-facing teams (any size)' and the '30 USD per user per month' price. He concludes by emphasizing that this tier empowers "any individual within your organization that can actually participate in helping solve problems for customers." The camera alternates between wide shots of the engaged audience and closer shots of Jeetu, with the Cisco Live! logo consistently visible in the top right corner.

event_blocks.summary: At Cisco Live!, Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses and a dark blazer, presented dynamically on a circular white stage under blue and green ambient lighting, with an attentive audience watching. He emphasized that "AI is pervasive in every aspect of the Webex Platform," a statement mirrored on a large curved video screen displaying the 'AI Assistant's' integration across Webex Suite, Cisco Devices, Webex Control Hub, and Webex Contact Center. Jeetu detailed their goal for a unified AI assistant for all Webex properties, specifically mentioning "Webex Suite," "Cisco Devices," and "Webex Control Hub" for managing consoles, before the screen displayed the title 'Cisco AI Assistant for Webex Suite.' He then demonstrated a scenario where a participant, Clarissa, stepped away from a meeting, with the platform detecting her absence and providing a recap upon her return, a feature he highlighted as extending to messaging and Vidcast. Jeetu further explained that transcripts would capture non-verbal cues like emojis and actions, as shown on a 'Transcript' interface displaying 'Clarissa steps away...' and emoji reactions. He then shifted focus to IT administrators, stating AI makes their experience magical, introducing the 'Bringing everything together for IT' slide which outlined a comprehensive IT ecosystem managed through 'one single administrative management plane, which is called the Webex Control Hub.' Jeetu highlighted Control Hub's industry-leading capabilities and demonstrated its user interface, revealing integration with 'network performance insights with Meraki.' Finally, he introduced the 'Cisco AI Assistant for Control Hub,' illustrating its use by querying the assistant about 'Donna Miller's quality' and receiving diagnostic details about choppy audio due to packet loss, with the integration of ThousandEyes providing further network segment analysis.

Jeetu Patel.description: A bald man with clear-framed glasses, wearing a dark blazer over a black shirt, standing on a circular white stage at Cisco Live!. He holds a small black device and gestures expressively while speaking into a headset microphone, explaining the functionality of the Cisco AI Assistant for Control Hub and detailing a scenario involving a senior executive, Donna Miller, experiencing choppy audio.

Curved Video Screen Display.description: A large, multi-panel curved video screen above the stage that displays the speaker’s image and presentation slides. In this segment, it first mirrors the detailed transcript interface, showing a marketing meeting discussion, then transitions to a vibrant title slide 'Reimagining the IT experience with AI', and finally displays the 'Bringing everything together for IT' slide, visually supporting Jeetu Patel's presentation on AI's pervasive role and its benefits for IT.

Cisco Live!.description: The event branding visible in the top right corner of the screen, with a stylized logo and consistent presence throughout the segment, reinforcing the event's identity. It remains static and unchanging during the entire minute.

Stage Lighting Setup.description: Dynamic lighting with blue and green hues, including spotlights and ambient stage lighting, creating a professional and immersive environment. The lighting accentuates the speaker and the stage, contributing to the professional atmosphere.

Innovation Theater.description: A large, dimly lit auditorium with rows of seated attendees and multiple large screens displaying presentation content, with blue and green lighting accents. The stage is circular and elevated, with the speaker positioned centrally, and the audience is visible in wide shots, indicating high engagement with the presentation.

Webex Suite.description: The software platform being demonstrated and discussed by Jeetu Patel, highlighted for its AI-powered features like automatic meeting recaps and summarization capabilities across its messaging and Vidcast components.

Presentation Slide.description: A slide displayed on the curved screen featuring the text 'Delivering great customer experiences' with three components: 'Proactive communications', 'Self-service', and 'Human engagement', each with corresponding icons (e.g., WhatsApp, Facebook, email, chatbots, Instagram, Messenger, and a headset). Below, the 'Webex Platform' is shown with a layered icon, visually connecting the three components.

AI Assistant Interface.description: A software interface titled 'AI Assistant' with a blue circular logo, displaying prompts like 'Let’s create some access rules today, shall we?' and providing options to create single or multiple rules, shown within the Cisco Secure Access dashboard.

Transcript Interface.description: A digital interface displayed on the large screen, featuring a 'Transcript' section with real-time meeting notes, including messages like 'Clarissa steps away...' and emoji reactions such as a heart and clapping hands. The interface also shows a search bar and a list of participants with their reactions. Jeetu Patel explicitly describes how this interface will capture not just spoken words but also non-verbal cues like emojis and applause, making the transcript more comprehensive.

Bringing everything together for IT Slid.description: A presentation slide displayed on the curved screen, titled 'Bringing everything together for IT,' which outlines a comprehensive IT ecosystem including Webex Control Hub, Cisco Devices, Webex Contact Center, Analytics Platform, and Data Lake, with data sources like User identity, Device identity, Workspaces, Network telemetry, and Energy utilization. Jeetu Patel uses this slide to introduce the concept of a unified IT management plane.

Audience Members.description: A diverse group of attendees seated in rows, many wearing lanyards with badges, dressed in business casual to formal attire, actively watching and reacting to the speaker. The audience is visible in wide shots, indicating high engagement with the presentation.

Webex Control Hub Interface.description: A user interface displayed on a large screen, showing an 'Overview' dashboard with user engagement metrics and a 'Cisco AI Assistant' panel. It visually demonstrates the AI Assistant's diagnostic capabilities by responding to queries about meeting issues for Donna Miller, displaying detailed meeting diagnostics, audio quality timelines, and network path visualizations, as explained by Jeetu Patel.

Network performance insights with Meraki.description: A presentation slide displaying a detailed dashboard with client connection diagrams, signal quality graphs (SNR and RSSI), and access point details, including status, MAC, SSID, LAN IP, DNS, and DHCP information. Jeetu Patel explicitly states that this represents the integration of network performance insights with Meraki into Control Hub.

Cisco AI Assistant for Webex Control Hub.description: A panel within the Webex Control Hub interface that greets the user and offers guidance. It is shown in action, responding to Jeetu Patel's hypothetical query about 'Donna Miller's quality' or 'any issues that she's having,' providing a summary of potential choppy audio due to packet loss, and offering a detailed 'Meeting diagnostics' view with network path visualization.

raw_segments: The video opens with Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses and a dark blazer, dynamically presenting on a circular white stage at Cisco Live!'s dimly lit auditorium. Blue and green ambient lighting illuminates the stage and vertical neon strips, while an attentive audience watches. Jeetu, holding a small black device and speaking into a headset microphone, gestures expressively as he explains that "AI is pervasive in every aspect of the Webex Platform," a statement mirrored on the large curved video screen above him. He elaborates on how "all of this is built on a core fabric of AI," emphasizing that "AI is pervasive throughout the entirety of the platform." As he speaks, the screen transitions to a diagram illustrating the 'AI Assistant's' integration across the Webex ecosystem, including Webex Suite, Cisco Devices, Webex Control Hub, and Webex Contact Center. Jeetu continues with enthusiasm, detailing their goal to "make sure that we have once again a unified AI assistant for all of the properties of Webex," specifically mentioning "Webex Suite," "Cisco Devices," and "Webex Control Hub" for managing consoles. The screen then displays the title 'Cisco AI Assistant for Webex Suite' on a vibrant blue-to-orange gradient. Jeetu confirms this, stating, "that AI assistant is going to be called the Cisco AI Assistant for the Webex Suite," before excitedly promising, "Let me show you a quick demo that's going to blow your mind. This is very cool." Throughout his animated delivery and strong eye contact with the audience, the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains visible in the top right corner.

Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer, and black shirt, stands on a circular white stage at Cisco Live!, holding a small black device and gesturing expressively into a headset microphone. He enthusiastically explains a Webex feature, setting up a scenario where he steps away from a meeting because his doorbell rings. As he speaks, the scene transitions to a screen demonstration of the Webex platform, showing a multi-person video meeting. The text 'Be right back' appears, and a notification pops up: 'We detected that you've stepped away from the meeting. You're muted and your video is off,' accompanied by a steaming coffee cup icon, visually illustrating Jeetu's narrative about a participant named Clarissa stepping away. Upon the participant's return, a recap notification appears: 'Catch up on what you've missed while you were away from the meeting,' followed by a summary of discussion points, which Jeetu highlights as a key benefit. The audience responds with applause to his rhetorical question about the feature's coolness. The video then cuts back to a wide shot of Jeetu on stage, continuing his presentation in front of a large, curved screen displaying the recap interface, with the audience visible in the dimly lit auditorium. He emphasizes that this summarization capability extends to the messaging platform and Vidcast, before the video concludes with close-ups of Jeetu, gesturing as he elaborates on the Webex AI Assistant's features, which are also shown on the curved screen with slides like 'AI is pervasive in every aspect of the Webex Platform' and 'Cisco AI Assistant for Webex Suite'.

The segment opens with Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer, and black shirt, standing on a circular white stage at Cisco Live! under dynamic blue and green lighting. He holds a small black device, gestures expressively, and speaks into a headset microphone, engaging the visible audience. Jeetu's voice explains that the transcript will capture more than just spoken words, stating, "you will even know in the transcript on what transpired besides just the um um just the script... the transcript will have Clarissa stepped away, there was an emoji, there were people that applauded. All of those non-verbal cues will actually be something that'll be available in the transcript for you." This aligns with a close-up visual of a digital 'Transcript' interface on a large screen, showing real-time meeting notes like 'Clarissa steps away...' and emoji reactions. The camera then pulls back to reveal Jeetu speaking in front of a massive curved video screen that mirrors this interface, displaying a detailed transcript of a marketing meeting. Jeetu continues, expressing excitement about AI capabilities, noting, "you should expect these to be at a very high velocity of innovation that'll continue to keep getting launched and delivered to you." The scene cuts to a vibrant title slide, 'Reimagining the IT experience with AI,' before returning to Jeetu, who continues his presentation center stage with vertical blue light strips in the background, emphasizing, "it's not just for the end user. We also care about the IT administrator in making their lives easier with AI... we make their experience magical with AI." The final frames show a new slide titled 'Bringing everything together for IT,' outlining a comprehensive IT ecosystem, while the Cisco Live! logo remains consistently visible in the top right corner.

The segment opens with a close-up of a presentation slide titled 'Bringing everything together for IT,' which outlines a comprehensive IT ecosystem, including Webex Control Hub, Webex Suite, Cisco Devices, Webex Contact Center, an Analytics Platform, and a Data Lake with various data sources. The scene then transitions to a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium with blue ambient lighting, where Jeetu Patel, a bald man in a dark blazer over a black shirt, stands on a circular white stage, gesturing expressively as he speaks into a headset microphone. The massive curved video screen behind him displays the same 'Bringing everything together for IT' slide, and his image is mirrored on side screens. Jeetu Patel articulates that 'everything together for IT' is achieved by having a common data lake platform, an analytics platform, and then Webex Suite, Cisco Devices, and Webex Contact Center, all managed in 'one single administrative management plane, which is called the Webex Control Hub.' He emphasizes that Control Hub is not just one of the best in the collaboration industry but also in enterprise software, making it hard to find 'amazing management tools like Control Hub.' The camera cuts to a close-up of Jeetu, who holds a small black device and continues to gesture, explaining that the audience is seeing an example of Control Hub, where everything can be managed from a single location. The presentation continues with a new slide titled 'Webex Control Hub,' showing a user interface with an 'Overview' section indicating 'Total engaged users: 4500' and a graph of user engagement. Jeetu then reveals they've 'integrated that with network performance insights with Meraki,' as the next slide, 'Network performance insights with Meraki,' displays a detailed dashboard with client connection diagrams and signal quality graphs. He concludes by stating, 'let's take it a step further,' introducing 'the Cisco AI Assistant for Control Hub,' as the final slide, 'Cisco AI Assistant for Webex Control Hub,' appears with a vibrant gradient background. Throughout, the 'Cisco Live!' logo remains in the top right corner, and the attentive audience is visible in the darkened auditorium.

The segment opens with Jeetu Patel, a bald man in a dark blazer, black shirt, and clear-framed glasses, standing on a circular white stage at Cisco Live! He holds a small black device and gestures expressively while speaking into a headset microphone, his voice clear as he asks, "So what does that do exactly? What would you do with the Cisco AI Assistant for Control Hub?" He then explains a scenario: "Let's say that you actually had word from someone as an IT administrator that this senior executive Donna Miller in your organization has actually been having some bad quality issues for some reason." The video transitions to a screen recording of the Webex Control Hub interface, displaying an 'Overview' dashboard with user engagement metrics like 'Total engaged users: 4512'. The 'Cisco AI Assistant' panel appears on the right, greeting the user, and Jeetu continues, explaining how one can "just simply ask the AI Assistant, 'Hey, how is Donna Miller's quality?'" or "'Show me any issues that she's having.'" The interface then shows the AI Assistant responding to the query "Show me any meeting issues for Donna Miller," confirming that "during a meeting, Donna Miller had some choppy audio" due to a 31% device audio packet loss. Jeetu emphasizes that the assistant goes a step further, showing "exactly where that choppy audio is" with a detailed 'Meeting diagnostics' view, displaying a timeline of the audio quality issue and a network path visualization with metrics like latency and jitter. He concludes by stating, "We integrate that with ThousandEyes, so ThousandEyes is then going to be able to provide you with details," as the interface continues to display the diagnostic information, pinpointing the problem to a specific network segment.

event_blocks.summary: At Cisco Live!, Jeetu Patel, wearing a dark blazer over a black shirt and clear-framed glasses, stood on a circular white stage, speaking into a headset microphone and holding a black device, to demonstrate the Webex Control Hub interface. The interface displayed a Cisco AI Assistant network diagnostic alert for user Donna Miller, showing 31% audio packet loss, with a ThousandEyes visualization identifying the 'Local ISP network' as the issue. Patel explained that the system could pinpoint network problems, whether caused by the ISP, app, device, or network itself, highlighting the ability to ask questions in natural language and receive responses in charts. He then shifted focus to sustainability, announcing new Control Hub capabilities and a 'low carbon mode,' which was visually represented on curved video screens displaying device energy consumption metrics (309K kWh, 72 tCO2e) and the highlighted 'New! Low Carbon Mode' feature. After Patel's presentation on the collaboration platform's innovation, applause and upbeat music followed as Oliver Tuszik, in a gray suit, took the stage. Tuszik thanked the speakers, declared Cisco's portfolio the 'best ever,' and addressed sustainability, assuring the audience of support from a 6,000-strong service expert team, all while the 'Cisco Live!' logo remained visible and the stage was lit with vertical blue and purple neon lights.

Sustainability.description: A presentation slide displayed on the curved screen, featuring the title 'Sustainability' and a dashboard with device energy consumption metrics, including 309K kWh total consumption and 72 tCO2e estimated carbon emissions. The slide also lists key sustainability features: 'Circular design principles,' 'Carbon Emission Insights,' 'Device Office Hours,' and 'New! Low Carbon Mode,' with the latter highlighted in green. Jeetu Patel introduces this as a major focus area and announces the addition of new sustainability capabilities to Control Hub, specifically a 'new low carbon mode'.

Curved Video Screen Display.description: A large, multi-panel curved video screen above the stage that displays the speaker’s image and presentation slides. In this segment, it shows the 'Sustainability' dashboard and the list of sustainability features, all with vibrant gradient backgrounds and clear white text.

Webex Control Hub Interface.description: A user interface displayed on the large screen, showing an 'Overview' section with 'Total engaged users: 4512' and a graph of user engagement, along with a sidebar menu including 'Monitoring,' 'Management,' and 'Account' options. The interface also features a 'Cisco AI Assistant' panel that interacts with the user by providing meeting diagnostics and network path visualizations, which Jeetu Patel describes as pinpointing issues and responding to natural language queries with charts.

Cisco Live!.description: The event branding visible in the top right corner of the screen, with a stylized logo and consistent presence throughout the segment, reinforcing the event's identity. It remains static and unchanging during the entire minute.

Jeetu Patel.description: A bald man with clear-framed glasses, wearing a dark blazer over a black shirt and dark trousers, energetically presenting Cisco's broad capabilities in networking, security, observability, and collaboration on a circular white stage at Cisco Live!, before handing over the stage to Oliver Tuszik.

Cisco AI Assistant for Webex Control Hub.description: A presentation slide with a vibrant gradient background (blue to orange) and white text, titled 'Cisco AI Assistant for Webex Control Hub,' indicating the next topic in the presentation. The assistant is shown in action, responding to queries with detailed meeting issue summaries and network diagnostics, which Jeetu Patel explains can pinpoint network issues and respond to natural language questions with data in charts.

Innovation Theater.description: A large, dimly lit auditorium with rows of seated attendees and multiple large screens displaying presentation content, with blue and green lighting accents. The stage is circular and elevated, with the speaker positioned centrally, and the audience is visible in wide shots, indicating high engagement with the presentation.

Oliver Tuszik.description: A bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, who takes the stage with a warm demeanor, thanking Jeetu and other speakers, praising Cisco's 'best portfolio ever,' and passionately discussing the importance of sustainability and the support provided by a team of over 6,000 service experts.

Audience Members.description: A diverse group of attendees seated in rows, actively watching and reacting to the speakers with applause, indicating high engagement with the presentations at Cisco Live!.

Stage Lighting Setup.description: Dynamic lighting with blue and purple hues, including spotlights and ambient stage lighting, creating a professional and immersive environment. The lighting accentuates the speaker and the stage, contributing to the professional atmosphere.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a close-up of the Webex Control Hub interface, showcasing the Cisco AI Assistant's network diagnostic alert for user Donna Miller, which indicates a 31% audio packet loss exceeding the 8% threshold. A ThousandEyes visualization highlights the 'Local ISP network' as the problematic segment, while Jeetu Patel, a bald man with clear-framed glasses, wearing a dark blazer over a black shirt, speaks into a headset microphone from a circular white stage at Cisco Live!. He explains that "the network path is actually going to show you why that issue occurred," confirming that "in this particular case it was a local ISP that caused the issue," though it could also be "the app," "your device," or "the network." He emphasizes the system's ability to "pinpoint exactly where that issue is for the network administrator," noting the "beauty about all of this is I'm asking questions in natural language and I'm getting back responses in charts." The scene transitions to a wider shot of Jeetu on the dynamically lit stage, gesturing expressively to the audience, as the presentation slides shift to 'Sustainability'. He announces, "Another big area that we actually focused on is sustainability," and explains that Control Hub will "add more capabilities for sustainability," specifically announcing "a new low carbon mode for sustainability." The curved video screens above him display a dashboard with device energy consumption metrics (309K kWh, 72 tCO2e) and a list of features including 'New! Low Carbon Mode' highlighted in green. The camera pulls back further, revealing the full scale of the Innovation Theater with its large screens and engaged audience, as Jeetu concludes, "So folks, that's the entirety of the collaboration platform, tremendous amount of innovation in both..."

The segment opens with Jeetu Patel, a bald man in clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer, black shirt, and dark trousers, energetically walking across a circular white stage at Cisco Live!. He gestures expressively with his hands while speaking into a headset microphone, holding a small black device, as he highlights Cisco's extensive capabilities across networking, security, observability, and collaboration, expressing excitement to bring these to the audience. The stage is illuminated with vertical blue and purple neon light strips, and the camera pans to show the vast, dimly lit audience, bathed in blue light, before cutting back to Jeetu as he announces he's handing over to his 'good friend Oliver.' As Jeetu concludes, applause erupts from the engaged audience, followed by upbeat, energetic background music. Oliver Tuszik, a bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, then walks onto the stage with a warm demeanor, gesturing openly. He thanks Jeetu and all the speakers, passionately declaring Cisco's offering as 'the best portfolio ever.' Oliver then transitions to discuss the crucial topic of sustainability, acknowledging it as a challenging project but assuring the audience of support from a dedicated team of over 6,000 service experts. He moves confidently across the stage, occasionally pausing to emphasize points, with the same vertical neon lights in the background, as the camera captures wide shots of the stage and the attentive audience. The 'Cisco Live!' logo remains consistently visible in the top right corner throughout the professional and immersive presentation.

event_blocks.summary: Oliver Tuszik introduces Adele Trombetta at Cisco Live!, who takes the stage, illuminated by purple and blue neon lights, to address the audience about sustainability, emphasizing the need for visibility into sustainability performance, referencing an IDC study, and highlighting the low percentage (18%) of EMEA IT departments tracking sustainability baselines. Adele, dressed in a light beige blazer, discusses challenges such as translating data into insights, adopting the right technologies, and managing budget pressure, illustrated by a 'Challenges' slide showing cracked earth, while advocating for profitable growth through sustainability initiatives and aligning technology with sustainability goals, showcasing Cisco's Silicon One and AI. The presentation transitions to slides detailing sustainability goals for 'Data centers' (40% less power capacity, 23% less operational cost) and 'Smart buildings' (50% less power consumption and cost), with Adele reinforcing these figures, emphasizing employee experience, and highlighting Cisco's role in digitizing the grid across Europe. She introduces the concept of 'Your sustainability journey,' displaying a winding mountain road and a detailed diagram titled 'Shape your sustainable technology roadmap,' outlining organizational and technology readiness, including strategy, governance, execution, IT for sustainable business, sustainable IT, enterprise IT, value streams, and ESG data, all under the 'Cisco Live!' banner in the Innovation Theater, with the audience attentively watching under blue lighting.

Oliver Tuszik.description: A bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, holding a microphone, speaking passionately on a dark stage with vertical neon light strips in purple and blue; he gestures expressively and moves dynamically across the stage, engaging the audience with a warm demeanor, and is heard introducing 'my colleague Adele Trombetta' with a clear, engaging voice.

Curved Video Screen Display.description: A large, multi-panel curved video screen above the stage that displays the speaker’s image and presentation slides. raw_segments: The video opens with Oliver Tuszik, a bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, standing on a dark stage illuminated by vertical neon light strips in purple and blue. He gestures expressively with both hands raised, then clasps them together as he speaks, his voice introducing "my colleague Adele Trombetta, who's running customer experience and service teams all around the mirror to explain you how she can help you." He moves dynamically across the stage before walking off to the right. The camera follows as Adele Trombetta, a woman with short brown hair, glasses, and a light beige blazer over a turtleneck, walks onto the circular white stage from the left, gesturing with her right hand as she approaches the center. The camera pulls back to reveal a large, curved video screen behind her displaying her name and title: 'Adele Trombetta, SVP and General Manager, CX EMEA, Cisco'. The audience, visible in the darkened auditorium, erupts in applause and cheering. The screen then transitions to a vibrant, animated graphic of a green, tree-like network. Adele stands center stage, speaking with an animated expression and gesturing with open hands, her voice clear as she greets the audience: "Hello, hello everyone, it's great to be here with you all." She then introduces her topic, "So now, topic is sustainability. You have heard a lot about that, but the point is, where do we start? How do we start? Well, we start with an X-ray." She continues, explaining, "I know it may sound strange, but before X-ray, what happened underneath the human body was invisible to the naked eye. With X-ray, suddenly the invisible became visible. And this is exactly how we should manage sustainability. Why? Because not everybody has visibility into today's sustainability performance. Actually, we've done a study with IDC, and..." A lower-third graphic appears, confirming her name and title. The camera alternates between close-ups of Adele and wide shots of the stage, showing the audience and the large screen displaying her live feed alongside the animated background. Blue spotlights illuminate the stage, creating a professional and immersive atmosphere, with the 'Cisco Live!' logo remaining in the top right corner throughout.

event_blocks.summary: Adele Trombetta, SVP and General Manager, CX EMEA, Cisco, presented a three-stage framework for sustainability, beginning with 'Shape your sustainable technology roadmap' and emphasizing building an actionable roadmap. She then moved to the 'Scale your transformation' stage, highlighting improvements in energy consumption through real-time observability, workplace digitization, and process automation across industries like data centers and smart buildings. Finally, she introduced the 'Sustain your journey with actionable insights' stage, focusing on digital dashboards providing real-time data, insights, and recommendations for power usage and carbon emissions. Following a brief visual of cracked earth, Petek Ergul, CTO, Global Head of Service Management at HSBC, discussed how automation contributes to better customer experiences, operational efficiencies, and achieving net-zero energy goals. The narrative then shifted to customer testimonials: Umar Ahmed, Director, Global Head of Network Architecture at Deutsche Bank, detailed their collaboration with Cisco to reduce carbon footprint and increase renewable energy usage in global data centers. A group discussion in a conference room reinforced the theme of renewable energy, while a man in a gray sweater was shown with a digital overlay of green foliage. Colin Bannon, CTO of BT, explained the significant electricity reduction and performance increase achieved through smart buildings, benefiting both sustainability and costs. Eman Al Awadhi, Vice President, Network & Cyber Security at Expo City Technology, affirmed Cisco's role as part of Expo's family, driven by shared values and a commitment to sustainable solutions. The segment concluded with Oliver Tuszik and a female speaker on stage at Cisco Live!, introducing 'CX customer heroes' like Yasser M. Alkholaif, Petr Haviček, Sandra Huber, Fatima AlDaghar, Petek Ergul, Eman Al Awadhi, and Anders. Tuszik discussed customer adoption and the sustainability journey, while the female speaker elaborated on 'Cisco Lifecycle Services,' detailing how they help improve sustainability, reduce costs, enhance security, and improve compliance, as illustrated on a presentation slide.

The scene transitions to a vibrant, split-screen graphic titled 'Scale your transformation,' showcasing 'Data centers' with server racks, 'Smart buildings' with green trees and modern architecture, and 'Industry solutions' with wind turbines. The camera then pulls back to reveal Adele Trombetta, SVP and General Manager, CX EMEA, Cisco, standing confidently in a light beige double-breasted blazer on a circular stage, her image mirrored on the massive curved screen behind her. She gestures expressively as she speaks, introducing the final stage: "Last is sustain. You have to sustain your journey," and highlighting the use of "amazing and digital dashboards that will give you real-time data, insights, and recommendations." This aligns with the presentation slide titled 'Sustain your journey with actionable insights,' displaying a dashboard with metrics for Power usage, Carbon emissions, and Recommendations. After Adele invites the audience to "hear from our customers" and asks to "run the video," the scene briefly cuts to a close-up of cracked, dry earth, symbolizing environmental challenges. The video opens with a close-up of a man in a blue shirt and glasses, seated in a modern office, as a female voice states, "Now we're working together with Cisco to drive this transformation with a greater purpose," echoing the on-screen text. The scene transitions to a woman in a green blazer speaking passionately in a minimalist room, with text indicating, 'We are working with Cisco on our sustainability strategy'. Umar Ahmed, Director, Global Head of Network Architecture at Deutsche Bank, then speaks directly to the camera in a well-lit office, elaborating, "We are working with Cisco on our sustainability strategy for our global data centers. With their expertise, we will reduce our carbon footprint, more importantly, increase our usage of renewable energy." This is followed by a group of four professionals in a conference room discussing around a table, visually reinforcing the theme of increasing renewable energy usage, before a man in a gray sweater is shown typing on a laptop outdoors with a digital overlay of green foliage. Alkholaif, Petr Haviček, Sandra Huber, Fatima AlDaghar, Petek Ergul, and Eman Al Awadhi—each associated with a sustainability or operational achievement, set against a dark blue background with floating geometric shapes. She responds, "Absolutely right," and, referring to the 'Cisco Lifecycle Services' slide now displayed on a large screen—titled 'Simple, flexible, outcomes-focused' with abstract blue and yellow wave patterns and the 'Cisco Live!' logo—she explains how these services can help "improve sustainability," "reduce costs," "enhance security," and "improve compliance," directly referencing the scrolling benefits listed on the slide.

event_blocks.summary: At Cisco Live!, Adele Trombetta announced her team's readiness with "6,000 experts" and partner support, transitioning the stage to Oliver Tuszik who introduced Fletcher Previn, Cisco's CIO, as a 'customer zero' for new initiatives, particularly AI infrastructure. Fletcher explained the 'customer zero' role and the challenge of building a high-performance AI cluster using Cisco Ethernet and RDMA, supported by a 'Deployment Architecture' slide detailing a complex network for 'Mountain View (MTV)' with Cisco and SONiC components. A host then elaborated on the technical specifics: a 400-gig leaf and spine network with 32 nodes and 256 Nvidia H100 GPUs, connected via 400-gig optical links, a 100-gig storage network, and a 100-gig in-band network, with 10-gig out-of-band management using Cisco ISRs, Nexus 9Ks, and Cisco 8000s, humorously noting Cisco's tendency to 'over-provision'. A subsequent slide, 'Building a Cisco AI Cluster', showcased Cisco 8000 series, Silicon One devices, Nvidia DGX servers, and Cisco's 400G optics, emphasizing its operational status. Oliver Tuszik and Jeetu Patel then discussed the cluster's functionality, with Jeetu confirming Cisco runs "all our AI stuff on this one," enabling employees to use AI tools like Chat GPT and co-pilot via a secure, private tenant where prompts are controlled, logged, and audited, ensuring data privacy. Jeetu further detailed leveraging LLMs with retrieval augmented generation and proprietary information for product information and case notes, while Oliver inquired about AI's use for productivity. Jeetu confirmed AI integration in Webex suite, AI Ops, and Catalyst Center, automating tasks like radio attenuation from 'once every couple of months' to 'every 30 seconds', all presented within the energetic Innovation Theater under dynamic blue and purple stage lighting.

Oliver Tuszik.description: A bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, speaking passionately on a dark stage with expressive hand gestures and a warm demeanor, standing next to Jeetu Patel. He asks Jeetu a question in a clear, slightly accented voice about whether his team uses AI internally for productivity.

Deployment Architecture.description: A detailed technical slide from Cisco Live! displaying a network diagram for 'Mountain View (MTV)', showing components like Storage Network, Compute Network, and OSM Network, with a legend identifying Cisco and SONiC hardware and optical modules. The slide is actively described by the host, detailing the 400-gig leaf and spine network, 32 nodes, 256 Nvidia H100 GPUs, 400-gig optical connections, 100-gig storage and in-band networks, control plane, 10-gig out-of-band management with Cisco ISRs, Nexus 9Ks, and Cisco 8000s as the Ethernet backbone.

Building a Cisco AI Cluster.description: A slide with six images: Cisco 8000 series Silicon One devices with blue and yellow cables, an elevation view of AI cluster network fabrics, a front-side view of an Nvidia DGX compute server, a rear-side view of the same server with yellow cables, and Cisco's QDD-400G-DR4-S 400G optic providing network connectivity. This visual is central to the discussion, referred to as the 'blueprint' for Cisco's 'AI stuff,' which enables internal use of tools like Chat GPT and co-pilot, implemented securely within a private tenant.

He holds a small black device in one hand and gestures expressively with the other while speaking into a headset microphone, moving dynamically across the stage and engaging the audience with animated hand movements and direct eye contact. He speaks with a clear, professional tone, discussing the integration of LLMs with retrieval augmented generation and proprietary information for AI applications, and detailing how his team uses AI in the Webex suite, AI Ops, and Catalyst Center to automate tasks like attenuating wireless access point radios from monthly to every 30 seconds.

raw_segments: The video opens with a wide shot of a large, dark auditorium at Cisco Live!, where Adele Trombetta, a woman in a beige blazer, stands on a circular white stage alongside Oliver Tuszik. Behind them, a large curved screen displays 'Cisco Lifecycle Services' and its tagline 'Simple, flexible, outcomes-focused,' with a scrolling list of benefits. Adele gestures expressively as she speaks, confidently stating, "6,000 experts in my team and tens of thousands expert with our partners, we have got your back and we are ready to start. Let's go." The screen then transitions to a bold, repeating message: 'WE'VE GOT YOUR BACK' in bright blue text. As the camera pulls back to reveal the vast audience and the immersive stage setup with blue lighting, Adele exits. Oliver Tuszik, a bearded man in a gray suit, takes center stage, walking confidently while gesturing and holding a small device. He speaks passionately about customers, saying, "I want to end this session with a customer close to me... this is a special one... you will understand in a second why he's a special one." The lighting shifts to include green and yellow accents as he introduces, "Please welcome on stage, Cisco's CIO, Fletcher Previn!" Energetic music plays as a man with curly hair and glasses, wearing a dark blazer, walks onto the stage from the side. Oliver greets him, "So, it's good also to have you on stage, finally," and Fletcher responds, "Thanks for having me." The two men greet each other warmly, shaking hands and engaging in conversation. The stage is illuminated with dynamic lighting, and large screens display their images and presentation content, including Fletcher Previn’s name and title, creating an energetic and professional atmosphere.

The segment opens with a wide shot of a dimly lit Cisco Live! auditorium, where Oliver Tuszik, a bearded man in a gray suit, speaks animatedly on a circular white stage, gesturing with both hands. He addresses Fletcher Previn, who listens attentively in a dark suit, asking in a distinct non-native English accent about their role as 'customer zero' for new ideas, specifically introducing the topic of AI infrastructure. The camera pulls back to reveal the massive curved screen above the stage displaying Fletcher Previn’s name and title. Close-ups show Oliver continuing to speak with expressive hand movements and a warm smile, before Fletcher Previn, identified by a lower-third graphic as 'SVP and Chief Information Officer, Cisco', begins to speak with hands clasped. In a clear American accent, Fletcher explains the 'customer zero' concept, highlighting the challenge and excitement of doing things for the first time. He then details the need to build a high-performance AI cluster of GPUs using the Cisco Ethernet stack, aiming to prove its capability, create shared infrastructure across the company, and develop a reusable blueprint, mentioning the use of RDMA. The scene transitions to a detailed technical slide titled 'Deployment Architecture', illustrating a complex network diagram for 'Mountain View (MTV)' with various Cisco and SONiC components, including storage, compute, and management networks, branded with 'Cisco Live!' and a 2024 copyright notice, visually supporting Fletcher's technical discussion.

The video opens with a detailed technical slide from Cisco Live! titled 'Deployment Architecture', showcasing a complex network diagram for 'Mountain View (MTV)'. A male host in a dark suit, speaking with a clear and articulate voice, immediately begins to describe the diagram, explaining the connection between GPUs and the cluster. He details the 400-gig leaf and spine network with 32 nodes and 256 Nvidia H100 GPUs, connected by 400-gig optical links to a 100-gig storage network and a 100-gig in-band network. He further elaborates on the control plane and the 10-gig connectivity for out-of-band management, mentioning Cisco ISRs for out-of-band, Nexus 9Ks, and Cisco 8000s as the Ethernet backbone. He humorously notes that as a Cisco employee, he's allowed to 'over-provision'. The scene then transitions to a live stage presentation where the host, gesturing expressively, stands alongside a bearded man in a gray suit (Oliver Tuszik) before a large audience under dark blue and purple stage lighting, with the Cisco Live! logo visible. The video then cuts to another slide titled 'Building a Cisco AI Cluster', displaying six images of Cisco 8000 series Silicon One devices, Nvidia DGX compute servers, and Cisco's QDD-400G-DR4-S 400G optics with prominent blue and yellow cabling. The host continues, emphasizing, 'This is real, we have built this,' and encourages viewers to 'look at all that beautiful cabling and fiber,' directly referencing the images on the slide.

In a dimly lit Cisco Live! auditorium, two presenters, Oliver Tuszik and Jeetu Patel, command a circular white stage under blue and purple lights. A massive curved screen behind them displays a 'Building a Cisco AI Cluster' slide, showcasing Cisco 8000 series, Silicon One devices, AI cluster network fabrics, Nvidia DGX servers, and Cisco's 400G optic. Oliver, a bearded man in a gray suit, gestures expressively, explaining that the AI cluster is "up and running" and they've created a reusable blueprint. Jeetu, a bald man in a dark suit and clear-framed glasses, holding a small black device, responds with animated hand gestures, emphasizing the "real" and functional nature of the setup. He states that Cisco runs "all our AI stuff on this one," enabling every employee to utilize AI tools like Chat GPT and other models. Oliver then details the secure implementation, confirming Chat GPT and co-pilot are available to employees via a private tenant, where all prompts are controlled, logged, and audited, ensuring no data leaves the corporate network or is used for public training, all executed in a "safe and secure way." The camera dynamically alternates between wide shots and close-ups, capturing their energetic discussion, with the Cisco Live! logo consistently visible.

The segment opens with a close-up of Jeetu Patel, a bald man with clear-framed glasses, dressed in a dark blazer over a light blue collared shirt, speaking passionately on a dark stage with dynamic blue and red vertical light bars. Wearing a headset microphone, he gestures expressively, explaining how they are leveraging "the power of an LLM, combine it with techniques like retrieval augmented generation and use some of our proprietary information to really add value" for things like product information and case notes. The 'Cisco Live!' logo is visible in the top right. The camera then pulls back to a wide shot of the circular white stage, revealing Oliver Tuszik, a bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, standing opposite Jeetu. Oliver, also gesturing, asks, "are you using this in your team AI to be more productive, faster, better?" As the two engage in a dynamic conversation, walking slightly toward each other, Jeetu confirms, "Yeah, for sure. We are taking advantage of the productivity benefits of things like AI in the Webex suite, AI Ops and things like Catalyst Center," elaborating on automating tasks such as attenuating radios on wireless access points from "once every couple of months" to "every 30 seconds by itself." The audience is visible as silhouettes under blue lighting in the foreground, while large screens above display the speakers' live feeds, all within the professional and energetic Innovation Theater, enhanced by dynamic blue and purple stage lighting.

event_blocks.summary: At Cisco Live!, Oliver Tuszik, dressed in a gray suit, initiated a discussion on sustainability, questioning the budget allocation for this initiative. Jeetu Patel, in a dark blazer, responded that no extra budget was provided but acknowledged the expectation to deliver. Jeetu then detailed the practical necessity of pursuing sustainability in IT, highlighting the significant power increase required for AI GPU clusters (60-70 kilowatts per rack) compared to typical data centers (5-10 kilowatts). The presentation unfolded on a circular white stage within a large, dimly lit auditorium, featuring dynamic vertical light bars and a massive curved screen displaying speaker close-ups, all under blue spotlights and consistent with Cisco Live! branding. Oliver and Jeetu engaged in a dialogue about Cisco's integrated portfolio, with Oliver asserting the company's end-to-end capabilities and Jeetu emphasizing the value of a unified platform in simplifying IT operations and reducing complexity. Oliver, holding a small device, explained Cisco's approach to complex challenges like hybrid work, encompassing remote access, connectivity, device management, productivity, cybersecurity, and SD-WAN. The session concluded with Oliver thanking the audience and promoting his upcoming 'AI, Big Data, & Humanity: Delivering on the Promise' keynote, met with enthusiastic applause and upbeat music.

Oliver Tuszik.description: A bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, speaking passionately on stage with expressive hand gestures, holding a small device, and standing beside Jeetu Patel. His distinct voice, with a slight accent, adds to Jeetu's points and delivers closing remarks, thanking the audience and inviting them to return. The curved screen also identifies him as President of Cisco EMEA, presenting a keynote on 'AI, Big Data, & Humanity: Delivering on the Promise'.

Jeetu Patel.description: A bald man in a dark suit over a black shirt, speaking into a headset microphone with animated hand gestures, standing on the circular white stage and engaging the audience. His articulate voice discusses the complexity of fragmented solutions and the benefits of an integrated platform.

Their engagement is evident through enthusiastic applause and cheering at the end of the segment.

The stage is circular and elevated, with the speaker positioned centrally, and the audience is visible in wide shots, indicating high engagement with the presentation.

Curved Video Screen Display.description: A large, multi-panel curved video screen above the stage that displays the speaker’s image and presentation slides. In this segment, it shows Oliver Tuszik's live feed alongside the 'Guest Keynote' slide, which includes his name, title, and the session topic 'AI, Big Data, & Humanity: Delivering on the Promise' with the time 'Tomorrow 17:15'.

raw_segments: The segment opens with Oliver Tuszik, a bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, speaking passionately on a dark stage with dynamic vertical light bars shifting colors behind him. He gestures expressively while holding a small device, asking in a clear voice, "So, let's jump to the next hot topic, sustainability... did you get an extra budget to deliver on this one?" The Cisco Live! logo is visible. As he speaks, he turns and walks left, where Jeetu Patel, a bald man in a dark blazer over a black shirt, enters the frame, smiling. Jeetu takes over the presentation, responding in a measured tone, "Uh, I I did not get an extra budget to deliver this," to which Oliver interjects, "I assume it's still expected that you deliver." Jeetu then elaborates, explaining that beyond being important for the planet, there are practical reasons for the IT department to aggressively pursue sustainability, citing the significant power increase from 5-10 kilowatts per 42U rack for typical data centers to 60-70 kilowatts for high-performance AI GPU clusters. The camera pulls back to reveal a wide shot of the circular white stage in a large, dimly lit auditorium filled with an engaged audience, illuminated by blue spotlights and large screens displaying close-ups of the speakers. Jeetu continues to speak with animated hand gestures, moving slightly, while Oliver stands beside him, holding his device and listening attentively, occasionally nodding. The atmosphere is professional and energetic, reinforced by deep blue tones and colorful vertical light bars consistent with the Cisco Live! branding.

The video opens with a wide shot of a circular stage at Cisco Live!, bathed in dramatic dark blue lighting and accented by vertical neon light bars in red, green, and blue. Oliver Tuszik, a bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, stands on the right, gesturing animatedly while holding a small device. He speaks with a confident voice, explaining how to get "as much power as possible everywhere else from the top of rack down," confirming that "it is something where we're..." Jeetu Patel, bald and in a dark suit, listens attentively on the left, his hands clasped. Jeetu then interjects with an inquisitive tone, stating, "But you need so you need to do it anyhow because otherwise you would not be able to manage energy cost and energy capacity." Oliver quickly agrees with "Exactly." The camera alternates between close-ups of Oliver speaking passionately and wider shots showing both men. Jeetu then playfully challenges Oliver, asking if he's "customer zero" and "not allowed to buy anything else than Cisco, correct?" Oliver, with a smile, confidently replies, "Well, I why would I need to buy anything else? It doesn't even occur to me to buy anything else." Jeetu then poses a more serious question to Oliver, asking for his advice to "all the CIOs, the IT leader in the room" on how to "get the benefit out of the entire Cisco portfolio." Oliver begins to elaborate, referencing "a complex end-to-end problem like hybrid work" and listing various related challenges such as "remote access, survivable connectivity, device management, productivity, cyber security, SD-WAN." Throughout, the Cisco Live! logo remains visible in the top right corner, and the audience is seen seated in rows around the stage, engaged in the professional and immersive presentation environment.

The video opens with a close-up of Jeetu Patel, a bald man in a dark suit over a black shirt, speaking animatedly on a circular white stage at Cisco Live! He gestures with his hands clasped, then turns to his right, gesturing toward a large screen displaying abstract green and blue visuals. Jeetu's articulate voice discusses the complexity of fragmented solutions, stating, "You heard Jonathan talk about the value of the platform and G2 talk about how fragmented some of these spaces are... That's a lot of complexity... if I don't have to be the integrator... and I can just take advantage of the entire integrated platform, that's saving me complexity and making the IT job a lot simpler." The camera pulls back to reveal a wide shot of the expansive auditorium, filled with a large, attentive audience seated in rows under dim blue lighting. The stage is illuminated by spotlights, and a massive curved screen behind the stage displays Jeetu’s image and presentation content. Oliver Tuszik, a bearded man in a gray suit and light blue shirt, joins him on stage, holding a small black device and gesturing expressively while speaking, engaging with Jeetu. Oliver, with a distinct voice and a slight accent, adds, "Perfect, I think there's nothing more to add on such a summary. Driving the complexity down with a portfolio that goes end-to-end." He then thanks the audience, "Thanks to all of you for joining us. The show will continue and I'm looking forward to seeing you all tomorrow. Have a great day." The camera alternates between close-ups of Oliver and wider shots showing both men interacting. The curved screen then displays a slide for a 'Guest Keynote' featuring Oliver Tuszik, President of Cisco EMEA, with the title 'AI, Big Data, & Humanity: Delivering on the Promise' and the time 'Tomorrow 17:15'. The audience responds with enthusiastic applause and cheering, followed by upbeat, energetic background music, as the professional and dynamic atmosphere, underscored by the Cisco Live! branding, continues.

The interface also features sliders for 'Object 1' match precision and 'Minimum event duration', and is shown in both grid and 'List View' modes.

global_entity_library.Cisco Security Cloud: A presentation slide titled 'Cisco Security Cloud' with four protection layers: 'User protection,' 'Cloud protection,' 'Firewall,' and 'Breach protection,' each with corresponding icons, arranged in a rectangular box with 'AI' at the top center, which later updates to 'Identity Intelligence'.
